reviews.text: At its core, it's a sincere story about acceptance and self-belief, encouraging us to leave ignorance and fear behind, all set to an excellent soundtrack.

reviews.author: rcross

reviews.text: They did a fantastic job with this remake! The new song in the credits was really good too. Everything important about the original is there, all the heart and depth, all the fun, all the great music. This is a very good translation of the original to this format. I'll definitely be buying this one on blu-ray.
reviews.author: morphinapg

reviews.text: Check out my full review @ Rating: C [this movie] had every chance to expand on its animated predecessor, and it wastes almost all of it. Beat for beat identical to the 2016 original, this remake trades vibrant, sun-drenched color for green screen sterility, turning musical numbers that once felt joyously alive into stiff, mechanical spectacle. The lead performance brings real warmth to the title role, and the central chemistry has its moments, but neither is enough to disguise a film that never once asks why it needed to exist.
reviews.author: Manuel São Bento

reviews.text: “A story can be retold with real water and real faces, but realism means nothing when the magic feels less alive.” I honestly walked out of this feeling disappointed more than anything. This isn’t one of the absolute worst Disney remakes I’ve seen, and there are performances, visuals, and cultural details that I appreciated. But it might be one of the most unnecessary. The original animated movie is only ten years old, still looks beautiful, still sounds great, and has not aged enough to need another version of the same story. That’s the biggest problem: this movie never gives me a convincing reason for why it exists.
reviews.author: MattisR3D

summary: Teenage Moana answers the Ocean's call and, for the first time, voyages beyond the reef of her island of Motunui with infamous demigod Maui on an unforgettable journey to restore prosperity to her people.

summary: After a criminal network kidnaps Wang Wei's daughter and the corrupt police refuse to assist him, Wei sets out on his own to locate her. Navin, a tenacious journalist whose wife has mysteriously vanished, is his only ally. In this explosive martial arts showdown, the unlikely duo fights the kidnappers ruthlessly driven by a furious vengeance.

title: Deep Water

reviews.text: What I learned from the 2016 film Sully and this movie is that if Aaron Eckhart is on your flight, I hope you're a good swimmer because that plane is going DOWN.
reviews.author: Bradym03

reviews.text: Had it all!A high-intensity, high-emotion thrill-filled film with lots of elements to keep you at the edge of your seat. This film will have you angry, smiling, and crying throughout the film. This film does an excellent job of making you root for characters while also hating others. This film has a little bit of everything that anyone can enjoy.
reviews.author: jarvis-5251579

reviews.text: There are bad cheesy shark movies and there are good cheesy shark movies. Fortunately this falls on the good side of the line. The film does dramatically slow down when it asks the audience to care about the unnecessary character backstory’s and conflicts. Luckily that stuff takes a backseat to the sharky goodness. Would Recommend
reviews.author: GodJester

reviews.text: “What if we took the sharks from sharknado and put them in a dramatic film that’s supposed to pull on people’s heartstrings?” And that’s how they came up with this movie. Acting was good, believability, was very much not. There’s no world in which sharks would behave like that. Not even close.
reviews.author: noah978

reviews.text: Melodramatic (derogatory) with odd choices. There is a 10 year old girl who says “my parents won’t get to see how I turn out.” A number of characters just die off screen and you just never see or hear from them again? And, oddly, too many sharks. HOWEVER! The plane crash sequence is incredible and bumps this up a star. Also, it’s kind of unintentionally hilarious, and I appreciate that.
reviews.author: CapnJazzHandz

reviews.text: A competently made, albeit formulaic disaster/shark movie. It's really heavy on melodrama; I'm talking swelling string music and everyone clapping when the boy gets the girl type melodrama. The sharks look fully CGI, bad CGI too. It’s a testament to Harlin's filmmaking that the thriller at the core of this film is engaging despite it all. Eckhart is a standout when it comes to acting and carries much of this film. Perfect for a flight, but the unearned sentimentality is really hard to swallow.
reviews.author: Chemical404

summary: A group of international passengers on a flight from Los Angeles to Shanghai is forced to make an emergency landing in shark-infested waters. The terrified group is forced to work together and overcome their differences if they hope to escape their sinking plane and the frenzy of sharks drawn to the wreckage.

title: The Odyssey

reviews.text: This movie was breathtaking. To be honest, I went to the cinema with pow expectations mainly because of the "bad" armors. But holy cow, what an amazing movie.

Having read the book, this just doesn't add anything. Yes the cinematography has its moments but in general Nolan seems startingly uncreative. It is just the same as a Harryhausen film but with CGI.
reviews.author: Joe_Diablo

reviews.text: But why? Bland, rambling, soulless. I really struggle to see the point of this movie other than for the sake of money. It seems to me that Nolan chose the cast based solely on names that are currently in vogue, rather than finding the best actors suited for the various roles. I've almost always enjoyed Christopher Nolan's films, but here he really disappointed me by trying to manipulate a story that he perhaps wasn't able to fully understand in all its complex facets.
reviews.author: LuisaMe

summary: Odysseus, the legendary King of Ithaca, embarks on a long and perilous journey home following the Trojan War. Throughout his voyage, he is forced to confront the whims of gods, mythological monsters, and trials that stretch both his cunning and his humanity to the breaking point.

reviews.author: GodJester

reviews.text: When the yellow perils manage to gatecrash a film studio and cause havoc on the set of a film, the director is terrified that his two rotund bosses will want his head. Instead, though, they adore the chaos and commission him to make more films featuring the "Minions". Swiftly, they go from rags to riches and are hugely popular until... Sound. Silent movies have gone the way of the dodo and now there is to be audio. Yikes - who wants to sit in a drive-in and listen to lots of squeaking and squawking coming from these diminutive stars? Well, nobody really and so swiftly they are back out on poverty row once more with nowhere to go.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

summary: This is the rambunctious, ridiculous and totally true story of how the Minions conquered Hollywood, became movie stars, lost everything, unleashed monsters onto the world and then banded together to try and save the planet from the mayhem they had just created.

title: Kraken

reviews.text: I am glad to see a Norwegian movie among many mainstream movies nowadays. The plot is quite interesting but sometimes not well explained. Deserves to watch it but low your expectations.
reviews.author: eiras

reviews.text: At the halfway mark of [this movie], it’s all slow buildup and still no interesting characters. Starting to wonder if the script or the performances deserve the blame, or if they’re working together on this. Given it's name, I expected more from this movie.
reviews.author: durack

reviews.text: Is it just me or was the lead's acting impossible to watch (Sara Khorami). This movie was already weak but having a lead who can't convey any emotions makes it fall even flatter.
reviews.author: NorskCanuck

reviews.text: The only thing that kept me from walking out of the theater is the fact that I'm shy and sat in the dead center of a packed premiere. To kickstart this horrendous experience they gave goodie bags to 4 random people out of the 100+ in the audience, and somehow I was one of the "lucky" few. I got a shitty bottle I will never use and a free coffee at a restaurant (I don't drink coffee). Just as a side note: I reserve the 1/10 rating for movies I don't bother finishing. Effectively making 2/10 my lowest "real" rating. Somehow this movie dodged that bullet.
reviews.author: Bac0nSandwich

reviews.text: After Trollhunter (2010) and the later Troll-movies (2022,2025) it is well established that Norwegian filmmakers are confident and proficient monster-builders. So also with this movie, which is a cautionary tale about meddling with nature. The casting is solid, the character-work leaves the protagonists and antagonists with motivations and relationships you can relate to, which means that the tension has stakes. The direction is good and the effects are just lovely. There are a lot of physical props to compliment the grander cgi-pieces, and it all blends very well together. I enjoyed this a lot.
reviews.author: Sierbahnn

reviews.text: [this movie] follows marine biologist Johanne working at a fish farm in a rural fjord community, where strange events and brutal deaths point to something lurking deep below. The idea is strong from the start, with a solid setup involving a sonic device meant to protect salmon that ends up awakening a massive hidden creature. The Norwegian setting is beautifully shot, and the film is at its most effective when the [this movie] itself appears. Despite the promising premise, the film struggles with limited effects, weak character development and several plot issues. The audience often knows more than the characters, which reduces tension, and the CGI can feel unconvincing at times.
reviews.author: JonDonnis

summary: A marine biologist is doing research on a fish farm when she encounters several strange occurrences. Along with the brutal deaths of two teenagers, all signs point to the deep fjord; can there be more to the depths than the eye can see?

The film is more than a tribute to Texas Chainsaw, the Hills Have Eyes, Last House on the Left and other video nasties of that era. It looks like it belongs with those films but with 40 years of foresight and technological advances. This will stay with you in a bad way.

reviews.author: chewy74

reviews.text: I stumbled across an add for this on Instagram I believe. From the brief glimpse I had seen it looked like something I’d be into. It was really nice to see Sean William Scott back on the big screen. I totally dug how they filmed this in 16mm. It really gave it that old school, grimy, slasher feel to it. The movie had some promising ideas but it never fully executed everything. The practical effects and make up design were excellent. This had a few incredible looking gory moments. The doll face look was really creepy and intimidating. It’s a serviceable low budget horror movie. It just moves a bit slow and slightly drags but it was decent enough.
reviews.author: robertaajr

reviews.text: Based on the segment Baby Girl from Horror Anthology Volume 1, [this movie] is a brutal independent feature that’s getting a cinematic release on 6th March. Director Rod Blackhurst has done this indie short proud, giving us a real tour de force of horror which proves the genre is not dependent on remakes and sequels alone. This is pure, unapologetic terror with a grindhouse feel and visuals that hark back to the golden age of horror. There are homages to rage fuelled classics we all adore and has a gritty intense vibe that Tobe Hooper would be proud of.
reviews.author: graeaehorror

reviews.text: A couple (Fabianne Therese and Seann William Scott) is trudging through the woods somewhere in the middle of nowhere. It’s unclear why they’re together. They certainly don’t have any chemistry. At some point, they run into a Leatherface wannabe in a puppet costume, and the so-called plot takes its course. It’s clear from the very first moments that '70s films like “The Texas Chain Saw Massacre” were obviously the inspiration for "[this movie]." Pure terror is definitely the focus. But for me, the retro look only worked to a limited extent.

reviews.author: patelritiq

reviews.text: I put it on knowing perfectly well what I was getting into. Spanish Wattpad adaptation, step-siblings romance, teen drama with mansions in the background. That's what it promises and that's what you get. Nicole Wallace and Gabriel Guevara are hot and have chemistry, I won't deny it. But the script gives them exactly zero interesting material. It's all intense looks at the pool, absurd fights, and a relationship that normalizes more red flags than a football match. I'm not going to pretend I expected anything else. If you like the genre and you've already seen everything from After... this will entertain you.
reviews.author: Aratea

reviews.text: Someone must have got fed up with the constant slating of the "After..." series and thought - "I know, let's make something that will really make those films look good". Guess what, they succeeded. "Noah" (Nicole Wallace) has had to relocate to live with her mother and her new husband in a palatial home that also happens to be occupied by her rather weedy step-brother "Nick" (Gabriel Guevara). Now the two do not hit it off. He's a bit of a cocky git and she's equally no-nonsense about things - so that ought to be the end. Rock and hard place kind of thing. Nope, no such luck.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: Culpa Mía is a passionate and tension-filled story that mixes family drama with forbidden romance. Nicole Wallace shines as Noah, a young woman who is forced to adapt to a new life in the mansion of her stepfather, William Leister, played by Iván Sánchez. There she meets her stepbrother Nick (Gabriel Guevara), with whom she maintains an intense and emotionally charged relationship, in which their rebellious and tormented personalities lead them to explore a connection that breaks family and social rules. The film combines moments of romance with adrenaline, highlighting the world of illegal racing, an element that intensifies the plot by adding constant danger for Noah and Nick.
reviews.author: Jkrilin

summary: Noah must leave her city, boyfriend, and friends to move into William Leister's mansion, the flashy and wealthy husband of her mother Rafaela. As a proud and independent 17 year old, Noah resists living in a mansion surrounded by luxury. However, it is there where she meets Nick, her new stepbrother, and the clash of their strong personalities becomes evident from the very beginning.

title: Greenland 2: Migration

summary: Paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren take on one last terrifying case involving mysterious entities they must confront.

title: Black Hawk Down

reviews.text: There are some movies that just stand out literally WAY above anything else. BHD is most definitely one of the best modern era war films I've seen and repeatedly watch.
reviews.author: Dudaai

reviews.text: Very accurate representation of the events that happened that day in Mogadishu. Many of the rangers in that battle where under 20 years old. The way they were able to adapt and keep fighting in a terrain of hostile militias was incredible. It’s unfortunate that the pentagon made a lot of strategic mistakes before the operation took place, many lives could have been saved. 8/10 for me
reviews.author: aron_sensation

reviews.text: >"Nobody asks to be a hero, it just sometimes turns out that way." I can't imagine how Ridley Scott went about filming this movie. It is meticulously chaotic, brutal and sad. While it is a war movie, I like that it is less about emotion and more about being a team and shines light on how the leaders at the time really messed this up.
reviews.author: Narate

reviews.text: The context of the film is both everything and nothing to this film - sure it helps to set the scene and plot, but the larger point the film seems to be making is that once all hell breaks loose, it is quickly forgotten in a battle for survival. Emphasis here is on the camaraderie amd day-to-day routines of the soldiers and the film does a great job of building tension up to the inevitable crash. What follows is a near two hour assault where the filmmakers immerse the audience in the midst of the battle.
reviews.author: SimonYNWA

reviews.text: _[this movie]_ is a fantastic war movie that is sure to keep you on the edge of your seat with its non-stop action. From the very beginning, the film throws you into the middle of the chaos, with intense and realistic battle scenes that make you feel as if you're right there on the ground with the soldiers. The action never lets up, and the tension continues to build as the soldiers face one obstacle after another in their mission. The film also does an incredible job of recreating the look and feel of Mogadishu.
reviews.author: VantageReel

reviews.text: Much better than "Pearl Harbor" but not as good as "Saving Private Ryan". Three war movies that are forever grouped in my head for coming out in a comparable time range, all with stellar casts. If you are a history or war buff, this one should be checked out! Rating: 3.5/5 - 80% - Would Recommend
reviews.author: Copeland1994

summary: When U.S. Rangers and an elite Delta Force team attempt to kidnap two underlings of a Somali warlord, their Black Hawk helicopters are shot down, and the Americans suffer heavy casualties, facing intense fighting from the militia on the ground.

title: Ready or Not: Here I Come

reviews.text: Doesn't reach the level of the first movie, some scenes drag on a little bit to long but it has some fun elements to it.

Grace has one chance to survive, keep her sister alive, and claim the High Seat of the Council that controls the world. Four rival families are hunting her for the throne, and whoever wins rules it all.

title: The Invite

reviews.text: [this movie] is a marriage comedy that's as uncomfortable as it is funny. Its characters are wonderfully written and perfectly cast. The confined setting occasionally makes scenes feel repetitive, but the dialogue keeps the story engaging. It explores heavier themes than expected, yet it remains fun, thoughtful, and entertaining.

reviews.text: It's a difficult thing to balance cringe/awkward humour without having it overstay its welcome. Marvelous performances and ability to thread that needle. Clever writing and a blast to see with an audience. Laughter was had by all. There are some tiny gripes, but as one setting movies go this is a great achievement. 7.5 and might even go higher with subsequent viewings.
reviews.author: boatoar

reviews.text: Extremely sharp, witty, funny and unexpectedly deep... this film was excellent. The performances from all four actors were sensational and completely faultless. It’s rare to hear genuine laughter in a cinema, but there was audible laughing all around the room. ‘It would be rude not to touch her boobs, Joe,’ then brutal dismantling of flawed relationships, perfectly captures the films ability to pull you pillar to post, love it.
reviews.author: CalmovieMunn

reviews.text: I loved this, absolutely phenomenal. Olivia Wilde once again proves her directorial prowess with her best work to date. The cast deliver performances that are on par with their best work truly inhabiting their characters adding gravity and depth to every frame of the film. The set design is a perfect container for this visceral, sharp, and incredibly funny dark human drama. Highly Recommend

title: Pressure

reviews.text: The idea that came to my mind while watching the movie was this. "We have always watched WW2 movies from the eyes of straight aces. This is different because we are watching it through the eyes of the generals right now." and I came to the following conclusion. I think more war films are needed "from the eyes of a general".
reviews.author: illetyus

reviews.text: Andrew Scott as meteorologist James Stagg does a lot of heavy lifting to raise this above just another mediocre WW2 movie. Even going as far as delivering a speech about way the weather is interesting. This is fine but not notably better than other similar movies.
reviews.author: GodJester

reviews.text: A story about weather forecasting on D-Day does not sound like an interesting one, and yet that is exactly what [this movie] is. Strong performances across the board and the weight of impossible decisions turn this into an effective chamber drama. While the film occasionally simplifies complex events and leans into dramatic confrontation, those choices rarely undermine its effectiveness. It may be a small-scale film, but the tension and significance make it feel much larger.
reviews.author: fasterfilmcritic

reviews.text: Weather is something that affects us all, though it’s also something to which most of us pay little attention unless there are potential or actual consequences at stake; then they often take on paramount importance. The impact can be somewhat ameliorated with an accurate forecast, but that’s often problematic, even with today’s advanced technology. So it can be hard to fathom what it might have been like to predict the weather without it, as was the case in the 1940s, when meteorology was just beginning to come into its own as an accepted science.
reviews.author: Brent Marchant

reviews.text: It´s kind of weird and sad, like in the twilight zone that US had once men like Eisenhower once and in 2026 it´s sink into to a maggot like Trump who can´t even read a book, finish a complete sentence or let alone win a real war or leas the US . The way thousands and thousands of Americans Lives were lost winning a battle only to become what they fought against.
reviews.author: Pedro

reviews.text: A fascinating film. Who could know just how critical the weather would be in the D Day landings. 👍 The tension keeps you on the edge of your seat... Is Group Captain Stag right or wrong? What a weight of responsibility for Stag and his meteorologists! Well worth a watch. 👍
reviews.author: Davezn

summary: In the tense 72 hours before D-Day, and the fate of the free world hanging in the balance, General Dwight D. Eisenhower and Captain James Stagg face an impossible choice—launch the largest and most dangerous seaborne invasion in history or risk losing the war altogether.

title: After We Fell

reviews.text: This is the point in the movie where everything goes haywire, veering off a cliff into confusion, leaving you wondering what you're even watching. Felt like a repetition without the depth of a storyline, filled with plot holes and rushed scenes that felt forced. If you're going to commit to a franchise, then commit wholeheartedly. Unfortunately, this installment falls short, making it the weakest link in the trilogy.
reviews.author: tmdb51616167

reviews.text: While not my favorite, this one is still up there. In this one, Hardin befriend Tessa's homeless father (as well as a junkie!). They bond together and drink, which turned out to be my favorite part of the movie. Some really great acting, especially when Hardin hands him money to go buy drugs, killing him in the process. He also takes the time to cheat on his girlfriend multiple times, while she is busy trying to mourn their relationship. He then keeps blaming her, in a masterclass of gaslighting. Fun for the whole family once again.
reviews.author: HitlerMalphite

reviews.text: I seem to recall seeing the previous episode of this trilogy in the cinema - a beneficiary of the lockdown dearth that propelled some serious dross onto the big screen. This, mercifully, never found a home there and so could be watched, half-heartedly, from the comfort of my own living room. The rather uninspiring, self-indulgent characterisations of "Tess" (Josephine Langford) and "Hardin" (Hero Fiennes Tiffin) continue to vacillate from loving to loathing each other for reasons that continued to escape me after the first two films.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: _[this movie]_ is a pointless, angsty teen sex drama that undoes all of the character development from the previous film. Picking up where the last film ended, the return of Tessa’s long-lost father is quickly swept aside and Tessa and Hardin return to breaking up and getting back together…yet again. Josephine Langford and Hero Fiennes Tiffin’s performances have gotten worse (if that’s possible) and half of the supporting characters have been recast with lessor actors. And after three films, the Tessa/Hardin relationship just isn’t interesting anymore; especial when it’s going in circles. Even the soundtrack is stale. Incredibly disappointing, _[this movie]_ is a meandering mess.
reviews.author: jarvis-8243417

reviews.text: Saw it in the cinema. Everything was predictable even before entering the theater. The best part of the experience was me screaming "NO, SHIT" very loudly after the big reveal. The structure of the movie was basically sex scene-conflict-sex scene-conflict-sex scene-conflict-sex scene-conflict-sex scene. And I'm not joking there were 5 sex scenes in this 1 hour and 48 minutes movie (just between the MCs O_O). The sex scenes weren't even filmed well. All the actors who were good were replaced. Charlie Weber was definitely the piece that was missing the most.
reviews.author: irine_elle

reviews.text: After viewing the last two films in this series, I vowed never to watch another. But I did and after seeing this, I really wish I hadn't. It's the worst by far and I have wasted my time once again. Fool me once Hardin, that's fine. Twice? Fair enough. But three times? It's time we [this movie] out as you are feeding me empty promises and boring lies. Sadly, there is one more film to go. Will I watch it? As a completionist, yes. I have to. I want to see all of these characters fall into a black hole of neverending torment - they deserve it because that is what they put me through.
reviews.author: Lee Brown Barrow

summary: Just as Tessa's life begins to become unglued, nothing is what she thought it would be. Not her friends nor her family. The only person that she should be able to rely on is Hardin, who is furious when he discovers the massive secret that she's been keeping. Before Tessa makes the biggest decision of her life, everything changes because of revelations about her family.

They did a pretty good job with the prosthetics, but they definitely have some extremely uncanny valley looks to them that I could not get over for the entire duration of the movie. reviews.text: This is lightning in a bottle. The film perfectly balances adventure, humour, and supernatural mystery without ever feeling messy. It feels fresh, fun, and endlessly rewatchable.

reviews.text: This is THE perfect movie I was looking for and I am so glad I found it when I did cuz boy was I surprised, it's non stop suspense from beginning to end, at first you might think it's another slow burning vengeance without many stakes and then you hit the 50 or so minute mark and everything keeps changing, everything I assumed would happen didn't happen and the movie completely throws me away. The movie moves slow initially but the pace picks up midway through and milks the comparatively low runtime dry.
reviews.author: Dulneth D. Perera

reviews.text: **10/10** _It's one of the best mystery movies I've ever had the privilege to see. I didn't have high hopes for it at first even though the ratings were pretty high, as ratings nowadays are very untrustworthy, they are either over-rated or else under-rated mostly, only a few sum of ratings are justifiable. This deserves it's good ratings. If you are someone who is looking for a movie that is in the same league as masterpieces like **Shutter Island**_ _then I'll definitely recommend this masterpiece._
reviews.author: Mirai Kazuya

reviews.text: Spanish thriller of the highest caliber. Psychological complexity over pure action. Multiple narratives continuously disrupt perception. Tension emerges not from CGI or action sequences, but from intellectual confusion. Mario Casas and Ana Wagener deliver subtle performances. Only weakness: too many twists in too little time. By the end, surprise loses impact. Still: film rewards your attention.
reviews.author: RedRudolf

reviews.text: So, I have to put some comment here because I'm truly impressed by the Spanish cinema and especially this movie. Mind-blowing experience, perfect twists and one of the best suspense ever in the movie. Highly recommended if you are searching for a movies with mysteries that for sure you cannot predict from the beginning. I found also one more director/writer who I'm waiting to make his next movie/project - Oriol Paulo.

summary: Remy, a rat, possesses a palate far more refined than that of his fellow comrades. He dreams of becoming a chef, one who creates rather than scavenges. When fate deposits him in the sewers beneath one of Paris’s most famous restaurants, he finds himself ideally placed to fulfill his dream. Forming an unusual alliance with a hapless young kitchen worker, Remy begins a daring culinary double life. As Remy pursues his vision, he must navigate the suspicions of the calculating Head Chef Skinner, the disapproval of Remy’s own colony, and the foreboding presence of renowned food critic Anton Ego, who strikes fear in the hearts of chefs all throughout France.

title: Finding Nemo

reviews.text: One of the best animated films I have ever seen. Great characters, amusing animation, and laugh-out-loud humor. Also, watch for the little skit shown after the credits. It's all great stuff that simply must be seen.
reviews.author: Dave09

reviews.text: Pixar at its peak. An absolute masterpiece. The water animation was revolutionary at the time and is still beautiful. The secondary characters are incredibly memorable (the vegetarian sharks, the surfer turtles, the seagulls...). Just keep swimming. Always.
reviews.author: Aratea

reviews.text: My favorite film from Pixar. It's got a heartwarming story and is animated so beautifully. And with Dory there are a lot of funny scenes too.
reviews.author: Agent24

reviews.text: Hands down the best Pixar movie to date. It is so funny and I know the film word for word. Ellen DeGeneres is the star of this film without a doubt, it's perfect!
reviews.author: SparklinDiamond

reviews.text: ★★★★½ (4.5/5) Bright, funny, and surprisingly emotional. The ocean world feels huge and alive, and the animation still holds up. It balances adventure with heart perfectly, and the characters are instantly lovable. Simple story, but it hits hard. Almost perfect.
reviews.author: fulltimewatcher

reviews.text: First time I have watched this in awhile and man does it hold up as one of my favorite animated films ever! So quotable, with great characters, and a wonderful color palette. Rating: 5/5 - 95% - Highly Recommend
reviews.author: Copeland1994

summary: Nemo, an adventurous young clownfish, is unexpectedly taken from his Great Barrier Reef home to a dentist's office aquarium. It's up to his worrisome father Marlin and a friendly but forgetful fish Dory to bring Nemo home -- meeting vegetarian sharks, surfer dude turtles, hypnotic jellyfish, hungry seagulls, and more along the way.

title: JUJUTSU KAISEN: Execution

reviews.text: This felt more like an “action” recap than a recap of the season. The new episodes are very flashy which is up to you on whether you enjoy that or not.
reviews.author: yay267

reviews.text: Absolutely loved this movie. Reviewed just enough to make me remember the tragedy of the Shibuya Incident before introducing the new parts of culling game. Would watch again.
reviews.author: zlectric

reviews.text: Did I just wake up from a fever dream? This is not how you do a movie or recap.. 60 min of damn near no dialogue to explain the random fight scenes being shoved into your eye holes. WTF
reviews.author: OutlierForLife

reviews.text: Whilst I do reckon that the cinema is a great place for this kind of vibrant and action-packed animation, it’s not so important for the stories here as this spends quite a while abridging what are quite clearly previous storylines before teeing us up for an introduction to the new curse-laden grudge matches that are to play out in the next series. Now that’s hardly new, but at least the “Demon Slayer” films try to present more stand-alone movies that offer us something a lot more self-contained.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: Please don't go to theatre to watch this one. They never mentioned it explicitly but this is only a recap of Shibuya incident. The first 40-50 mins is basically scenes from season 2 Shibuya incident and then final 20 mins is just explanation of what Culling games is and a little team up moments. This is a blatant cash grab by Mappa where they said its a new movie but actually just a recap of the entire show so far.
reviews.author: ehsanulhb

reviews.text: I usually don't leave any reviews, and this one'll probably be lost in here.... The 1st 40min are just a recap of the last part of S2 (Gojo Sensei Sealing, Mahito vs Itadori, Nanamin 😢)... And it's actually not a good recap, most of the substance from the series is missing, but it's just fine, I'll say.. like a 6/10. I wouldn't say it's good because you don't feel like the emotional impact you feel in the series... But the 2nd part, are the 1st 2 eps of S3 and boy, it's a bang. There are many things happening for those of us who can't wait for S3, and it feels freaking amazing....
reviews.author: Cristobal Bautista

summary: A veil abruptly descends over the busy Shibuya area amid the bustling Halloween crowds, trapping countless civilians inside. Satoru Gojo, the strongest jujutsu sorcerer, steps into the chaos. But lying in wait are curse users and spirits scheming to seal him away. Yuji Itadori, accompanied by his classmates and other top-tier jujutsu sorcerers, enters the fray in an unprecedented clash of curses — the Shibuya Incident. In the aftermath, ten colonies across Japan are transformed into dens of curses in a plan orchestrated by Noritoshi Kamo. As the deadly Culling Game starts, Special Grade sorcerer Yuta Okkotsu is assigned to carry out Yuji's execution for his perceived crimes. A compilation movie of Shibuya Incident including the first two episodes of the Culling Games arc.

title: Hunting Season

reviews.text: Gibson does Brosnan. A semi-slow burner not in any way a big production, but it will keep you on the edge of your seat.A decent rescue/revenge story.No matter what Mel is a legend!
reviews.author: jarvis-5251579

reviews.text: A low-budget, simple, old-style movie — but I liked it. Who doesn’t love it when the bad guys get what they deserve for doing bad things to good people? Mel Gibson is great, as always.
reviews.author: ragnarLothbrok

reviews.text: I enjoyed this movie. Some intrigue, some action, and retribution. I enjoyed the relationship between Bowdrie and Tag, a good solid family, with one lie from Bowdrie.. 🙈. Well worth a watch. 👍
reviews.author: Davezn

reviews.text: I expected a faster pace from this movie and more gunfights like the final action sequence. IMO, Sofia Hublitz was great in this. I don't think I have seen her work before this movie (I really need to add Ozark to my watch list).
reviews.author: durack

reviews.text: Solid, slow-burn, low budget Thriller with a good Mel Gibson, who levitates the production. The story doesn’t reinvent the genre, but it’s refreshing to see a decent movie without excessive use of CGI, without stretching the plot unnecessarily, but to keep it within 90 minutes, and without ignoring the physics in action scenes, but keeping the shootouts straight and short. A fair movie.
reviews.author: Monsieur FU

reviews.text: In the 80s and 90s, [this movie] would be in the box office top 10 rank on it's premiere. Nowadays, it's just one of the many streaming release. Too bad, there is no box office system implemented for streaming services due to the complex calculation of p&l. Anyway, considering how low budget this was, [this movie] did good with the storyline, script, acting and actions. Mel Gibson still has his star making acting prowess. Without Gibson, this film would fall under the radar. Because of his directoral experience, [this movie] has a very thick Gibson's signature influences. Letting the story played priority role instead of actions, violence, CGI or sexual scenes.
reviews.author: MikeRgr

summary: When a reclusive survivalist and his daughter rescue a mysterious, wounded woman from a river, they become entangled in a deadly web of violence and revenge, forcing them to confront a brutal criminal to survive.

title: The SpongeBob Movie: Search for SquarePants

reviews.text: Only reason this is actually decent is that it’s just a rip off of the old [this [this movie]] where SpongeBob and Patrick have to save the bikini bottom from plankton.

reviews.text: Felt like a drawn out episode than a [this movie].. I feel like this could’ve been accomplished in 22 minutes.. and was he basically playing the video game version of SpongeBob. Get to the big x and move on.. weird..

reviews.text: I found Sponge Out of Water to be the best one. The first [this movie] is then the second best. This [this movie] is great to look at and fun. It’s just lacking story wise but at least better than Sponge on the Run.

reviews.text: Watched this with my 8 year old who bloody loved it. Having sat through (I think) all of the SpongeBob films with him, I have to say I appreciated this as a massive step up from most of the previous entries in the franchise (from the perspective of a 43 year old man with no history with or nostalgia for the original cartoon). The humour is fairly base and in places it relies heavily on bums and bodily functions.
reviews.author: ambrudenell1

reviews.text: I thought this was more fun than the previous movies, but I am not into Spongebob, we went with our kids... That said, I have seen most of the movies, and this one was my favorite to date, maybe it was just Clancy Brown and Mark Hamill, but I just got a lot of laughs from this...
reviews.author: SpiderJerusalemInTheMountains

reviews.text: Only just tall enough to go on the big boy’s ride, “SpongeBob” and his best pal “Patrick” are lured onto the pirate ship of the dreaded “Flying Dutchman” and promised an exciting adventure before he is rewarded with his swashbuckling certificate. Meantime, “Mr. Krabs” races in hot pursuit because he knows that his enthusiastic, but gullible, young protégé is being lead on a perilous journey that could end up releasing untold terror on the high seas.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

summary: Desperate to be a big guy, SpongeBob sets out to prove his bravery to Mr. Krabs by following The Flying Dutchman – a mysterious swashbuckling ghost pirate – on a seafaring adventure that takes him to the deepest depths of the deep sea, where no Sponge has gone before.

title: Coco

reviews.text: One of my all-time favorite movies. Heartwarming, funny and touching movie that comes with a lovely message. Could rewatch it again and again. 10 out of 10!
reviews.author: LiYuna

reviews.text: god i was about to cry !! i think i already did, that's was so beautiful and so emotional really loved it and definitely will rewatch many times!! perfect!

reviews.text: Made me cry in my mom's arms. The last time i cried in my mom's arms was when i was a kid and didn't get a lollipop. I'm 21 years old now. 10 outta 10.

reviews.text: Sobbed my heart out like a baby multiple times. One of the most beautiful stories I have seen from Pixar. Cannot believe I slept on this for so long, but I'm glad I was finally ready to see it.
reviews.author: Poverty23

reviews.text: ★★★★★ (5/5) Beautiful, colorful, and surprisingly emotional. The music is great, but it’s the family theme that really hits. By the end, it’s hard not to tear up. It feels heartfelt and genuine the whole way through. Easy five stars.
reviews.author: fulltimewatcher

reviews.text: It’s an amazing movie! The story is incredibly captivating and emotional, and the graphics are so realistic. Everything about it is impressive, but for me, the story stands out the most. I would rate it 7.5 out of 10. Hector's heartbreaking story almost brought me to tears💖😓.
reviews.author: Mam′Cinephile

summary: Despite his family’s baffling generations-old ban on music, Miguel dreams of becoming an accomplished musician like his idol, Ernesto de la Cruz. Desperate to prove his talent, Miguel finds himself in the stunning and colorful Land of the Dead following a mysterious chain of events. Along the way, he meets charming trickster Hector, and together, they set off on an extraordinary journey to unlock the real story behind Miguel's family history.

title: Blades of the Guardians

reviews.text: This film is highly engaging. The martial arts sequences are exceptionally smooth and mesmerizing The visuals were magnificent. The balance of humor feels well-judged, adding lightness without disrupting the flow of the story.
reviews.author: jarvis-5251579

reviews.text: [this movie] is highly engaging from start to finish. The martial arts sequences are exceptionally smooth, stylish, and mesmerizing, while the visuals are absolutely magnificent. The humor is well-balanced, adding just the right amount of lightness without disrupting the story. The sandstorm fight sequence is easily the standout moment an absolute peak that showcases the film at its very best.
reviews.author: Gozmit24

reviews.text: "[this movie]" follows in the footsteps of what could be characterised, in terms of genre, as "Chinese Westerns". It's a similar formula to the spaghetti Western. A rough-hewn, lone wolf character, with a questionable past, who, overcoming their personal shortcomings and trauma, ends up doing the right thing by others in need. This film's single biggest claim to fame is action. It's relentless, with wave after wave of frenetic, polished martial arts action. Given this film is set in the classic period of China's long history, this is combined with swordplay and archery to good effect. By all means watch this film just for these elements; it's an amazing watch.
reviews.author: MovieGuys

reviews.text: Blades of the Guardian is simply a fun, entertaining adventure from beginning to end. At its heart is a familiar “escort mission” story, where a lone warrior must protect someone important while traveling through dangerous territory. Fans of films like The Gauntlet or Escape from New York will recognize the formula, while the relationship between the hero and the child brings to mind Lone Wolf and Cub. The movie throws a little bit of everything into the mix: fast-paced wushu combat, comedy, a touch of romance, mysterious swordsmen, and plenty of colorful supporting characters.
reviews.author: pkway

reviews.text: 9/10 stars review ratings from me; it is a well deserve review. It's been a long time since the movie 14Blades, Red Cliff etc. And 2026 is just the right year to have a great movie like this to appear. No nonsense fight scenes Great cinematography and it just feels right on every angle that you look at it. There is a few twist .. on the plot but not to heavy that makes you confuse. All in all, its a splendid watch.
reviews.author: Zeus

reviews.text: From Donghwa to Animated series to epic cinematic film, [this movie] is one hell of an epic story to be adapted. Yuen Wo Ping was wise to stick the core storyline with little deviation. Master Yuen carefully choreographed the fight and dramatic scenes to stay loyal to the core story. I did question the choice of Wu Jing playing Dao Ma since Dao Ma was very visually a calculative character, not an easy task for just another actor to play. In his prime, Tony Leung Ka Fai would be the perfect actor to play Dao Ma. But he is too old and fit better as Old Man Mo.
reviews.author: MikeRgr

summary: Dao Ma, the "second most wanted fugitive," is entrusted by his benefactor, the chief of Mo family clan, to take on a mysterious escort mission-escorting the "most wanted fugitive" to Chang'an.

title: Irreversible

reviews.text: Sabía que era una película difícil, pero joder la escena del túnel es terrorífica, horrible y muy dolorosa. La del extintor es fuerte pero merecida. una pregunta: cómo descubren quien es el tipo?

reviews.text: Deceived by 7+ rating I started watching. After 15 minutes I still forced myself to keep watching. After 30 minutes I had seen nothing worth to see, wasted half an hour of my life. What a dull obnoxious movie. Even the camera work is irritating.

reviews.text: Camera moving is amazing. Cast awesome. Gaspar Noé is a unique genious. Many layers, many different vibes. Shocking and cute at the same time. Def a hidden gem in the history of cinema. Honestly big respect. Btw Monica Bellucci is a goddess. I mean.... no wonders Vincent married her. I'd do the same.

summary: A woman’s lover and her ex-boyfriend take justice into their own hands after she becomes the victim of a rapist. Because some acts can’t be undone. Because man is an animal. Because the desire for vengeance is a natural impulse. Because most crimes remain unpunished.

title: Your Fault

reviews.text: Better than Culpa Mia in IMO.Whether you're a fan of action, drama, or adventure, this movie has something for everyone. So grab some popcorn, sit back, and get ready.

reviews.text: Who are his crazy characters? Gabriel became impressionable... he disappointed me despite his word at the end. I find it too easy. He wasn't honest with Noah, he kept blaming him... I hope the third one will be nicer to watch

reviews.text: Some very convenient writing. Some new characters, some from the past and just so many miscalculations. Not better than the first one in any case. It seemed that the equation between the leads just disappeared because Noah was just attracted to another man. The story just seemed off. Just a random average movie.
reviews.author: patelritiq

reviews.text: Second part... The problem with sequels that exist because the first one worked commercially, not because the story called for it. And well, couple that fights, reconciles, external obstacles that appear, they fight again. A script that seems incapable of imagining drama beyond the breakup and reconciliation cycle. Correct production, careful cinematography, soundtrack good for the emotions... But I felt everything like filler between the introduction of the first movie and the conclusion of the third.
reviews.author: Aratea

reviews.text: I was less enamored by this one. There were fun moments, some of the dramatic twists were fun, but it lacked something the first one had. The chemistry just didn't hit the same.
reviews.author: quintennyson

reviews.text: Culpa tuya is the second part of the Culpables saga and follows the romantic drama of Noah and Nick as their relationship faces even more complicated problems. With new characters, family conflicts and tense moments, the film maintains a good balance between romance and action. Nicole Wallace and Gabriel Guevara shine with a chemistry that continues to be the driving force of the story, while the rest of the cast perfectly complements the plot with interesting secondary characters. Although it doesn't stray too far from the clichés of the genre, it manages to keep fans hooked. If you enjoyed Culpa mía, you'll love this one.
reviews.author: Jkrilin

summary: The love between Noah and Nick seems unwavering despite their parents' attempts to separate them. But his job and her entry into college open up their lives to new relationships that will shake the foundations of both their relationship and the Leister family itself.

title: The Amazing Spider-Man

reviews.text: Visually slick, stylish, and very early 2010s in its vibe, but not much beyond that. Andrew Garfield brings charm, but the film feels unnecessary, a retread of the origin story without much to say. It has moments, but it’s mid overall.

The monster is really boring and weird-looking and that very unsubtle US flag shot and whole "working man's hero" didn't really do it for me.

I personally preferred the green goblin as a villain to the Lizard but both were portrayed really well. The CGI is better in this one though that is to be expected. but in conclusion I think both movies are on a level playing field.

summary: Peter Parker is an outcast high schooler abandoned by his parents as a boy, leaving him to be raised by his Uncle Ben and Aunt May. Like most teenagers, Peter is trying to figure out who he is and how he got to be the person he is today. As Peter discovers a mysterious briefcase that belonged to his father, he begins a quest to understand his parents' disappearance – leading him directly to Oscorp and the lab of Dr. Curt Connors, his father's former partner. As Spider-Man is set on a collision course with Connors' alter ego, The Lizard, Peter will make life-altering choices to use his powers and shape his destiny to become a hero.

title: 3 Idiots

reviews.text: I usually don´t like B movies, but I saw this one by chance and have to say, it was amazing. Would recommend 9/10, because of some cliché scenes.

reviews.text: [this movie] is too long and even though the story isn’t terrible, it’s just not that funny. Almost child like humor. Also don’t care for the musical numbers. Guess it’s just a cultural difference. I don’t hate it, but I can’t help but find this extremely mediocre.
reviews.author: JoeyvanAwesome

reviews.text: I've never been able to connect with Bollywood films, and unfortunately "[this movie]" is no exception. Maybe a runtime of about 90 minutes would have resulted in a more enjoyable film for me. But 164 minutes is just too much. Every joke is unnecessarily drawn out so that the punch line has no effect. Furthermore, the character development only happens at a sluggish pace, even though you know exactly where the journey is going. The biggest problem for me, however, was the main character, Rancho. He is nothing but a know-it-all, smug asshole. He bullies several people in the course of the film and never has to suffer any consequences for it.
reviews.author: AlfieSGD

reviews.text: I haven't watched many Indian movies because it's not exactly easy for me to be introduced to them. Besides, if the movie is not available in a streaming platform, finding decent subtitles in my language might be quite an undertaking, and we have to agree that watching a 170-min length movie requires some serious commitment. Still, I'm so glad I've found this precious gem among CineTrak's suggestions and decided to give it a go. Granted, not all of its arcs are great, but the important ones are superb masterpieces... even with some silly songs here and there. Hollywood could REALLY learn a thing or two with overseas' 7th Art (not only Indian) because this solid 9.0 movie is amazing!
reviews.author: magnusthorek

summary: Rascal. Joker. Dreamer. Genius... You've never met a college student quite like "Rancho." From the moment he arrives at India's most prestigious university, Rancho's outlandish schemes turn the campus upside down—along with the lives of his two newfound best friends. Together, they make life miserable for "Virus," the school’s uptight and heartless dean. But when Rancho catches the eye of the dean's daughter, Virus sets his sights on flunking out the "3 idiots" once and for all.

title: Back to the Future

reviews.text: They don't movies like this anymore, perfect in every way. One of my favourite ones of all times. Even after all these years still stands up just as great.

reviews.text: Everything is here to make this a great horror – a creepy setting, extensive backstory, a strong fan following, but something doesn’t quite hit right. Instead of playing to these strengths, the film leans more into horror tropes (jump scares, a lightening storm that strikes at the exact right moments, creepy pasta looking cg creatures). This really is a film for fans of the game. If like myself you've not played it, this adaptation feels like a rushed effort full of weird graphic choices and plot devices. It could do with time dedicated to giving everyone an opportunity to immerse in its world and enjoy the carnage that lies ahead.

reviews.text: This feels like propaganda specifically targeted for the MAGA crowd. I didn't know much about it going in, or I wouldn't have wasted my time. It's a conservative fantasy of what human trafficking looks like. I don't know why white evangelical Christians are so obsessed with this narrative that white girls are getting kidnapped off the streets. It's not happening. Yes, human trafficking is a problem, but not for affluent, white, American women. Sorry to burst your bubble. But feel free to enjoy the newest human trafficking porn. It wasn't for me.

reviews.text: The casting for this is not particularly noteworthy, with the exception of the main protagonist, and some of the camera work and writing is a bit underdeveloped. Some of the effects leave a bit to be desired as well, but I don't think that takes away from the enjoyment of this movie. This thing is still on point and the directing is more than adequate for what this wants to convey. It's sort of like Taken and Rambo in a weird mix, and I think that works well enough to be worth the investment of your time.

reviews.text: Not my first time to watch this one, and probably won't by my last. It gets some bad rap from other reviews but in all honesty, this is simply another of those _"disengage your brain and just enjoy the action"_ movies. Some of the stunts are completely eyeroll-worthy but this is still fun to watch. If you're looking for an adrenaline-rush movie that is almost laughably unbelievable, this is it...but it's still a fun action movie.
reviews.author: $hubes

reviews.text: This was always going to age like milk. It’s very much in love with its edgy take on James Bond, and with a director like Rob Cohen at the helm, the end results feel unsurprisingly meatheaded and juvenile. The story and characters are disposable and cliché, which is fine for what it’s trying to do. However, there’s no charm to any of the acting or dialogue, which I’d argue is pretty important if you want to make this kind of movie work. Instead, what we get is a lot try-hard cringe that feels like it’s really aiming for the early 2000s douchebag demographic.
reviews.author: Jordyep

title: Witness for the Prosecution

reviews.text: Like a lot of Agatha Christie's work, it starts to collapse once we get to the conclusion. However, the first 90 minutes of this are really strong. Loved the main character and all of the quirks given to him by the script and direction (blinding monocle; his love of cigars; the health pills which I was honestly expecting to become a major plot point at some point). The plot is engaging right from the start because of the compelling mystery and excellent direction. Wilder controls the amount of exposition by constantly cutting back to flashbacks, there's constantly this sense of breadcrumbs being dropped but it's unclear how the pieces fit together.
reviews.author: Jordyep

reviews.text: [this movie] is a twisty little devil. Charles Laughton is magnificent as Sir Wilfrid, and he carries the viewer to the end. This film is intelligent, but it lacks the comedy of Billy Wilders' other movies - and that comedy is missed.
reviews.author: fasterfilmcritic

reviews.text: Many people rate this as Christie’s best movie adaptation. I disagree and find several others to be better. It’s good of course but I much prefer Death on the Nile from ‘78 which is my favorite. It has great actors, much more interesting characters and possibly the best on locations sets of any film. As for this movie, Tyrone Power’s acting is pretty awful. The way he speaks makes it seem as if he is repeating memorized lines - which he is of course lol but he’s not supposed to show it. What’s great about this film IMO is Christie’s plot and not so much the movie itself. Plus court room dramas suck. There I said it.
reviews.author: hirkiti

reviews.text: I'm constantly surprised that women's hats don't provoke more murders. Leonard Steven Vole finds himself on trial for the murder of a wealthy widow from whom he has inherited a fortune. Top barrister Sir Wilfrid Robarts takes up the defendants case, but he, along with everyone else, is stunned when Vole's seemingly loving wife Christine turns up to testify against the defendant. Based on Agatha Christie's successful 1953 play, "[this movie]" benefited from fine tuning from master director Billy Wilder and writing partner Harry Kurnitz.
reviews.author: John Chard

reviews.text: Overly theatrical for modern tastes, a bit too reliant on building legal gotchas for Sir Wilfrid, and the great premise is diminished by the final sequences that goes overboard with the melodrama. With all of that said, I can see why it's one of the top rated movies of all time. Sir Wilfrid is a great character, lots of sharp dialogue, and quite the twist.
reviews.author: SkinnyFilmBuff

reviews.text: I might be asking for trouble here, but did Charles Laughton ever actually make a bad film? He leads this one as the brilliant but curmudgeonly barrister "Sir Wildred Robarts" with oodles of charisma and style. Marlene Dietrich portrays the evil, manipulative "Christine Vole" marvellously and even Tyrone Power, more the matinée idol than the serious actor, delivers as required in Billy Wilder's outstanding rendition of the Agatha Christie story about a man accused of murdering an elderly lady who has just happened to leave him a great deal of money in her will.

They exaggerated it so much, it's like in a Chinese movie where a guy jumps up in the air and doesn't come down – that's the level of exaggeration. I give it a 3 out of 10. I think they tried to make something fantastical.
reviews.author: Enes_

reviews.text: "Rather silly" is my first and lasting reaction to "[this movie]". It tediously recycles overused concepts to craft, if that's the right word, a predictable and oftentimes absurd, action espionage thriller. It's all about a group of super spy teens trying to escape the CIA, that created them. Worse still, the evil Russians are out to exterminate them, or are they? The action, such as it is, is reasonably well done, if implausible, and the acting is alright but not inspired. In summary, a very ordinary action spy thriller you have seen many, many times before in one form or another. A basic watch.
reviews.author: MovieGuys

reviews.text: This movie is so lazy. It is campy as hell and does not take itself seriously, but fails to be entertaining enough that it needs editing tricks to make it feel like something is happening. All those slow-mo action shots? Ew. The slow-mo tries to add the cool factor in tandem with Renee's apparent omniscient ability to know where every bad guy is.Seriously, those slow-mo shots are the tip of the iceberg, the sped-up shots are the real annoying thing. They too happen so much, but outside of the action scenes and makes no sense in their placement. One was in the middle of an establishing shot... for no reason.

reviews.author: GreenFrog221

reviews.text: Wacky, cartoonish martial arts action with a big CGI budget and rampant imagination, similar in tone to Stephen Chow's preceding work, Shaolin Soccer. It's very much a case of style over substance, as the plot only serves to move us from one outrageous set piece spectacle to the next, and makes no bones about it. That's Chow's style, with far more focus on effects-laden fighting techniques than inscrutable storytelling, and it works for him. We're treated to dozens of unique forms, from countless unexpected sources, as nearly every denizen of a dusty, lower-class slum turns out to be a kung fu master of some sort.
reviews.author: drqshadow

reviews.text: _"I didn't know the Lion's Roar...could be done with a speaker."_ Very underwhelming. Hell, that may even be an understatement. I went into this film expecting to be relatively entertained because so many familiar reaction channels on Youtube have reacted to this film and seemingly loved the hell out of it. Not only that, it seems like the majority of people, overall, feel the same way. Well, I don't. So, I pretty much think this film is massively overrated. Yeah, this film is super goofy and "fun." Different, especially, perhaps in a way no other films are. Or not many other films are. And that's great and all.

reviews.text: An animation classic, no doubt about it. This film oozes style with its sleek 60s spy aesthetic, distinct character designs, and a tone that balances action, family drama, and satire effortlessly. The world feels alive, the visuals still hold up beautifully, and the whole vibe is just timeless. It’s nostalgic, it’s creative, and it’s packed with memorable set pieces and characters. One of Pixar’s absolute best

reviews.text: *"We need an emergency extract from this fucking zoo! and that's not an option!"* It's what I expected to a degree–a b-movie version of *Jurassic Park* set in a warzone–but I wanted more camp and comedy out of that premise. Half of the time they play it pretty serious and the production has some decency to it too (minus the corny dialogue, accents and acting). The dynamic between the trio and the plot of returning the baby has a little heart, and there’s some early 2000s charm to the animation. But overall, it never really grabbed me, emotionally or visually. It’s got some okay ideas and a simple premise, but nothing that makes me want to revisit it.

reviews.text: Inventive and heartfelt, [this movie] brings a unique concept to life with vibrant animation and a solid sense of humor. It’s not the most emotionally deep film, but the core group of characters is well-established and pretty memorable. There’s a strong nostalgic charm to it, and even if the plot is fairly basic, it feels cohesive and fun. A solid start to the series.

reviews.text: This adaptation is definitely visually striking, it’s CGI-heavy, trippy, and full of that strange Burton-esque surrealism. I actually kind of enjoy that weird 2000s vibe it has, and it holds up pretty well in that department. But beyond the style, the characters are forgettable and the story’s not all that gripping. It doesn’t live up to the original or really justify its own existence, but I can still appreciate the attempt. Just not a favorite.

How I rate: 1-3 ❤ = seriously! don't waste your time 4-6 ❤ = you may or may not enjoy this 7-8 ❤ = I expect you will like this too 9-10 ❤ = movies and TV shows I really love!

summary: In the mystical Scottish Highlands, Merida is the princess of a kingdom ruled by King Fergus and Queen Elinor. An unruly daughter and an accomplished archer, Merida one day defies a sacred custom of the land and inadvertently brings turmoil to the kingdom. In an attempt to set things right, Merida seeks out an eccentric old Wise Woman and is granted an ill-fated wish. Also figuring into Merida's quest — and serving as comic relief — are the kingdom's three lords: the enormous Lord MacGuffin, the surly Lord Macintosh, and the disagreeable Lord Dingwall.

title: The Equalizer

summary: McCall believes he has put his mysterious past behind him and dedicated himself to beginning a new, quiet life. But when he meets Teri, a young girl under the control of ultra-violent Russian gangsters, he can’t stand idly by – he has to help her. Armed with hidden skills that allow him to serve vengeance against anyone who would brutalize the helpless, McCall comes out of his self-imposed retirement and finds his desire for justice reawakened. If someone has a problem, if the odds are stacked against them, if they have nowhere else to turn, McCall will help. He is The Equalizer.

reviews.text: Despite its title, there's nothing despicable in this film. Even the villain is charming in his own villain-y way. This movie has our beloved evil mastermind father returning as a not-so-evil superspy father. This is a light-hearted movie that sacrifices plot and complexity for simple jokes and fun. A good movie for any family, but don't expect to be too engrossed by its short attention span. 6.9/10 p.s. Minions are cute and funny. If anything, watch for them. Or not, they're pretty much everywhere anyways.
reviews.author: Anisound

reviews.text: Picking up right where the original left off, evil genius Gru has seen the error of his ways and transitioned into life as the single, adoptive dad of three young girls. The sudden reform has left everyone (an army of minions, a long-time collaborator and Gru himself) a bit stir-crazy, so he seeks new thrills as a turncoat, tracking and identifying his former rivals for a stuffy government agency. Illumination has leveled-up their equipment since the first film, which makes for a far more polished visual product, but the story is less lively and fuzzy than its surprising predecessor.

reviews.text: If you’re looking for a silver lining to [this movie]” trilogy, you could say that the three films managed to maintain their quality. The problem is that the quality was pretty lousy to begin with. By the third installment at the latest, even the most reluctant viewer should realize that there wasn’t a single halfway interesting idea behind this trilogy. Consequently, this finale feels aimless, especially since none of the characters behave in a way that makes sense and the whole small-town setting remains completely implausible. Ultimately, there is still only one “[this movie]” movie that’s truly worth watching, and that’s the first one. In my opinion, you can safely skip all the ones that came after it.

reviews.text: _[this movie]_ is a fantastic movie that has an original script, an intriguing storyline, and an outstanding performance by Tom Hanks. The movie provides a great insight into the psychological effects of loneliness and survival, taking you on an emotional rollercoaster. Hanks captures the character's emotional turmoil and physical struggle with outstanding realism. The cinematography is excellent, immersing you in the beauty and harshness of nature.
reviews.author: VantageReel

reviews.text: To make himself look like an average out-of-shape middle-aged man Tom Hanks didn't exercise and allowed himself to grow pudgy. Production was then halted for a year so he could lose fifty pounds and grow out his hair for his time spent on the deserted island. During this hiatus, Robert Zemeckis used the same crew to film What Lies Beneath (2000).
reviews.author: WhyNotCallMeRo

reviews.text: There's really a whole lot more to this than just Tom Hanks alone on an island. It works on a visceral level as a straightforward tale of survival amidst the harshest conditions imaginable, but underneath the surface there's all manner of allegorical goodness also at play. It's intense and powerful no matter how you choose to take it, though, and that's the sign of a real masterwork. That whole stack of cards teeters on Hanks's work in the central role, of course, and he flat-out crushes it.
reviews.author: drqshadow

summary: Chuck Noland, a top international manager for FedEx, and Kelly, a Ph.D. student, are in love and heading towards marriage. Then Chuck's plane to Malaysia crashes at sea during a terrible storm. He's the only survivor, and finds himself marooned on a desolate island. With no way to escape, Chuck must find ways to survive in his new home.

reviews.text: The fondness for this film is more for its nudity over anything else. It feels like a big budget skinamax flick with lots of hollywood regulars. I will not deny that the sex scenes were sexy. Sharon Stone oozes sex appeal in her role. But compared to Fatal Attraction, theres not much the director is adding to the visual storytelling. Its a risque pulp noir for the early 90s. I watched it till the end, but at alot of points, I lost interest. I wont completely dismiss the layering of manipulation and framing that occurred in this, but I have a hard time holding this to such a high pedestal as others have.

reviews.text: This is one of those movies you had to grew up with because watching it for the first time as an adult and it's so silly. The characters are stereotypes (the bullies, the manic pixie dream girl, the tough dad). I read it's a sad movie without spoiling myself as to why but the death comes out of nowhere my reaction was more "WTF?" than "Noooo!". And the CGI is so bad it takes you out. Pan's Labyrinth is thematically a similar movie and it's so much better.
reviews.author: The_Argentinian

reviews.text: _[this movie]_ attempts to explore childhood imagination and friendship, but is ultimately weighed down by forced drama, uneven storytelling, and a strangely hollow emotional core. The central conflict between the boy and his parents — especially his father — feels contrived. The father’s coldness is never explained in a way that makes sense for a child so young, and his occasional anger comes off as unearned and out of place. The subplot about the family being too poor to buy sneakers stretches believability. The film repeatedly insists on this hardship, yet the family owns a house, a car, and puts food on the table.
reviews.author: MacabreVerse

summary: Jesse Aarons trained all summer to become the fastest runner in school. So he's very upset when newcomer Leslie Burke outruns him and everyone else. Despite this and other differences including that she's rich, he's poor, she's a city girl, and he's a country boy the two become fast friends. Together they create Terabithia, a land of monsters, trolls, ogres, and giants where they rule as king and queen.

reviews.text: When an unexploded WWII bomb is discovered right next door to London's Paddington railway station, the police race to evacuate the locale and to cordon everything off ready for the army bomb disposal team. That unit is commanded by 'Maj. Tranter' (Aaron Taylor-Johson), a veteran of the conflict in Afghanistan, who swiftly sets up safe zones and informs the chief superintendent (Gugu Mbatha-Raw) that this is a live device that might take a while to make safe.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: I enjoyed the vibe of this one. Mackenzie was an outstanding director when he did Hell or High Water. Relay was a step down from that but still entertaining. This was similarly entertaining but also somewhat predictable in its twists. Serviceable and good one time viewing 6.5
reviews.author: boatoar

reviews.text: It may not reach the same level of suspense as David Mackenzie’s last film, Relay, but he still delivers a solid, engaging thriller here. The tension peaks during the sequence where police and military forces attempt to disarm a bomb in central London, and even as the story shifts beyond that, the film remains consistently gripping and enjoyable. Aaron Taylor-Johnson, Theo James, and Sam Worthington make for a strong ensemble cast that carries the film effectively. The pacing rarely lets up—just when you think you have a moment to breathe, it pushes forward again. While this brisk pace leaves little room for deeper character development, the ride is entertaining enough that it hardly detracts.
reviews.author: kwontuum

reviews.text: This movie is not bad, as it has its moments. Though it is also chaotic in that it tells actually two stories In parallel: a heist and a bomb squad story. Even if they bind the two stories together in the end, it does not really work. Both storylines are actually not bad on their own, but the weaving them together feels a bit chaotic and messy and distracts from either story. A pity, as I believe that it would have been better to make two good separate movies rather than one that feels medium.

reviews.text: Full review: Rating: B- "[this movie] is an effective exercise in emotional catharsis that survives its structural flaws thanks to a committed cast and Maika Monroe in a state of grace. It’s a film that reminds us that objective judgment in cinema is an illusion and that, sometimes, we just need a story that validates the need for a second chance. The imperfect beauty of redemption proves that, even on the grayest days, love is the only light capable of illuminating the shadows of our past."

summary: Wreck-It Ralph is the 9-foot-tall, 643-pound villain of an arcade video game named Fix-It Felix Jr., in which the game's titular hero fixes buildings that Ralph destroys. Wanting to prove he can be a good guy and not just a villain, Ralph escapes his game and lands in Hero's Duty, a first-person shooter where he helps the game's hero battle against alien invaders. He later enters Sugar Rush, a kart racing game set on tracks made of candies, cookies and other sweets. There, Ralph meets Vanellope von Schweetz who has learned that her game is faced with a dire threat that could affect the entire arcade, and one that Ralph may have inadvertently started.

title: The Pianist

reviews.text: I can’t believe it’s took me this long to watch this knowing about it for awhile because it’s picture is remarkable, deep, beautiful, emotional, eye opening and much more with a strong performance from brody that i’m not much of a fan off but he did a great job in this and he’s definitely made a place in my good books.

reviews.text: ★★★★½ (4.5/5) Quiet, restrained, and honestly hard to shake. Adrien Brody carries the whole film with barely any dialogue, and it works. reviews.text: Movies about Really Horrible Events like the Holocaust are sometimes overrated, in my opinion, because the seriousness of their subject matter overshadows the actual craft in making the movie. For me, this one is a bit of a mess–overly long, unfocused, poor writing, Adrien Brody, etc.
reviews.author: soonertbone

reviews.text: Very sad and depressing film based on a true story... and very eye opening also. Story about a Jewish [this movie] trying to survive during the autrocities during ww2..... great performance by Adrien Brody. Great directing as done by Roman Polanski..... I’m still thinking about this movie a day later.... some scenes were so sad and hurtful that they have remained in my mind. A must watch.
reviews.author: gunsgirl

summary: The true story of pianist Władysław Szpilman's experiences in Warsaw during the Nazi occupation. When the Jews of the city find themselves forced into a ghetto, Szpilman finds work playing in a café; and when his family is deported in 1942, he stays behind, works for a while as a laborer, and eventually goes into hiding in the ruins of the war-torn city.

reviews.text: [this movie] is unquestionably a classic horror story, but its idea is far better than its production. I really want to love this film, but it ranks last on the list of classic 80s horror movies.

summary: Teenagers in a small town are dropping like flies, apparently in the grip of mass hysteria causing their suicides. A cop's daughter, Nancy Thompson, traces the cause to child molester Fred Krueger, who was burned alive by angry parents many years before. Krueger has now come back in the dreams of his killers' children, claiming their lives as his revenge. Nancy and her boyfriend, Glen, must devise a plan to lure the monster out of the realm of nightmares and into the real world...

title: Road House

reviews.text: I love the original, and I really tried to go into this with an open mind, but I just can't find anything redeemable about it.
reviews.author: CharmedPagan

reviews.text: My jaw hurts from clenching my teeth throughout the movie. A lot of rage going on here. Meanwhile, my lady friend was watching 27 dresses. 😁

summary: Ex-UFC fighter Dalton takes a job as a bouncer at a Florida Keys roadhouse, only to discover that this paradise is not all it seems.

reviews.text: This is the coolest movie I've ever seen. From the dialogue, the needle drops, the anime, the action, and the comedy. It's all so hard. It almost makes me forgive Tarantino for his egregious feet shots. The best way to watch [this movie] for the first time.

La única escena que no me ha agradado mucho es la de los 100 ninjas, pero entiendo que sea una referencia a las películas de samurais. Le iba a poner 4.5 estrellas pero la poscreditos sumó el 0.5

summary: A former assassin, known simply as The Bride, wakes from a coma four years after her jealous ex-lover Bill attempts to murder her on her wedding day. Fueled by an insatiable desire for revenge, she vows to get even with every person who contributed to the loss of her unborn child, her entire wedding party, and four years of her life. After devising a hit list, The Bride sets off on her quest, enduring unspeakable injury and unscrupulous enemies.

title: Masters of the Universe

reviews.text: You could say the premise is pretty good for this kind of movie, and they did a few things right, but the execution falls through.
reviews.author: Makmende

reviews.text: I don't need a new one in 2026. This is a frickin' classic. Music score is on point. Plenty of action to keep me happy and THAT skeletor monologue... Pure retro gold.
reviews.author: sententious441

reviews.text: Great nostalgic movie. Music is on point! Amazing cast and doesn’t deserve the flack. Also just realised that Courtney & Christina are in both this and FRIENDS also!
reviews.author: mavrik316

reviews.text: It is far from a great film but Frank Langella as Skeletor is absolutely killing it! Definitely worth a watch just for his scenes alone.
