reviews.text: [this movie] aims for something abstract and thematic, but it never fully translates into a compelling experience. Scenes suggest meaning rather than build it, keeping the viewer at a distance. Visually, there is much to appreciate and the performances deserve respect, but the film rarely develops its ideas into something that carries weight. I applaud the beauty, but I left wanting more.

reviews.text: Extremely long. I do like films where the tense relationship between 2 characters is the focus of the story, but man... The first "big moment" of the film (plot twist/revelation/whatever, something that ups the stakes) comes after almost 1 HOUR of waiting for something to happen.

reviews.text: The eponymous rock star (Anne Hathaway - or perhaps Margot Robbie or Natalie Portman?) has had some sort of an incident with her erstwhile friend and fashion designer "Sam" (Michaela Coel) and so when she turns up unannounced at her home in the middle of a thunderstorm, she can't be too surprised that the reception she gets is just as wet. She needs a frock. A frock to end all frocks. She needs "Sam" to design it for her, as only she really understands her; knows her; yada yada...
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: I usually love A24 movies and I’m a big fan of Anne Hathaway, but this ended up being a plodding, tedious watch that literally put me to sleep several times. The central issue is that a film feels like the wrong medium for this kind of story. Two people talking in a room might work well on stage, but stretched into a two-hour movie, it just drags. Even strong performances from Michaela Coel and Anne Hathaway couldn’t save it, largely because of the script’s limitations. The concert scenes are well done and briefly sparked my interest, but they’re overshadowed by long stretches of artsy, empty, and overly metaphorical dialogue that doesn’t really lead anywhere.
reviews.author: kwontuum

summary: Long-buried wounds rise to the surface when iconic pop star Mother Mary reunites with her estranged best friend and former costume designer Sam Anselm on the eve of her comeback performance.

summary: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, the extraordinarily intelligent, silverback gorilla who has been in his care since birth. But a rogue genetic experiment gone awry mutates this gentle ape into a raging creature of enormous size. To make matters worse, it’s soon discovered there are other similarly altered animals. As these newly created alpha predators tear across North America, destroying everything in their path, Okoye teams with a discredited genetic engineer to secure an antidote, fighting his way through an ever-changing battlefield, not only to halt a global catastrophe but to save the fearsome creature that was once his friend.

reviews.text: Stunning film. Scratchy eerie score. So atmospheric. The sexual tension is palpable. The images are jaw dropping. Extreme close ups, imaginative wide angles. The changing state of Japanese society as it arrives into the modern world is melded with personal existentialism and relationship trauma. Simply one of the greatest films I have ever seen.
reviews.author: smallclone

reviews.text: *[this movie]* is a haunting psychological drama that explores freedom, isolation, and human resilience through compelling character development, striking black-and-white cinematography, and an immersive atmosphere. While its limited setting and restrained character backstories create some emotional distance, the film's powerful symbolism and thoughtful storytelling leave a lasting impression. Overall, it is a timeless classic that is highly rewarding for viewers who appreciate slow-burning, philosophical cinema. BY ALUNAUWIE
reviews.author: Alunauwie

reviews.text: Bizarre, beautiful mystery of a movie. I loved every second of it. Incredible photography, not only of the liquified patterns of moving sand, but of the cramped house that provides the main space of the movie. Reminds me a little of Picnic at Hanging Rock, in its singular vision and ethereal beauty.
reviews.author: soonertbone

reviews.text: - to me utterly & completely incomprehensable that this movie has been so neglected , Not included in - more 'popular' -watchlists ( or 'best movie' -lists ... ) - it is a Grand Feast for the eyes and the mind : the story , the acting , the photography is of such superb level !!! - i would hot hesitate to rate it ( - actually with many movie-'professionals' ... ) as among the top twenty best ( cinema-proper ) films ever made .

title: Ghost

reviews.text: A cinematic gem, [this movie] offers something for everyone—romance enthusiasts, supernatural fans, and thriller seekers. Its blend of laughter, tears, and suspense, coupled with memorable performances, makes it a timeless journey through life’s fleeting nature and love’s enduring legacy. Whether revisiting or discovering it anew, [this movie] remains a heartfelt reminder that true connection never fades.
reviews.author: Edgerk

reviews.text: Tony Goldwyn should have been a more popular actor. He was actually pretty exceptional in almost everything he has done but never actually made it to superstar status. Instead he kind of lingered in supporting actor limbo through the 90s and then kind of disappeared. It's a shame. And he's not the only unsung hero in [this movie], Vincent Schiavelli is in it too. It has a great cast that brings their all to the film. The script isn't that imaginative, you've seen a few movies like this before, but that never really matters when it's done well.
reviews.author: GenerationofSwine

reviews.text: "[this movie]" manages to successfully cut across a variety of genres, but unfortunately very few of them are used to particularly rewarding effect. The comedy doesn't even manage to raise the occasional chuckle, so there is a necessarily heavy reliance upon the romance. It undoubtedly soothes the heart broken amongst us to believe our loved ones are still dutifully watching over us with tender loving care and this film plays to that desire in people with a lot of help from The Righteous Brothers brilliantly singing the superb "Unchained Melody" on the soundtrack.
reviews.author: kevin2019

reviews.text: "Sam" (Patrick Swayze) and his girlfriend "Molly" (Demi Moore) aren't the best at pottery, but they do know how to make a mess! A trip to the theatre to see the Scottish play doesn't end well though, as he is randomly attacked in the street. She's desolate but fortunately his work colleague "Carl" (Tony Goldwyn) is on hand to try to help her through the aftermath. What she doesn't know, though, is that "Sam" is stuck in limbo. He can't move on and so must watch her every day life unfold.
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

It is one of the sleeper-movies that I didn't expect to be as good as it was. I recommend it.

reviews.author: Copeland1994

reviews.text: This one's worth watching, but not because it's riveting. It is a delightful accidental comedy. Between the story and the acting, you'll be on the edge of your seat thinking "this is ridiculous, maybe it's just this scene that's bad." A full watching would disabuse you of that notion. This movie is not good, and that is why it is worth watching. Oh and the end of the movie where they blatantly rip off the Saw franchise and have these rapid cuts detailing everything we didn't know before so the whole story suddenly makes sense? Chef's kiss.
reviews.author: brianleb

reviews.text: The aughts aren’t exactly known for having been a particularly good era for the horror genre. But as someone who actually liked “Saw,” the combination of director James Wan and screenwriter Leigh Whannell didn’t sound half bad at first. “[this movie]” was their joint follow-up project to “Saw,” so a certain level of anticipation is certainly not out of place. But after just a few moments, my excitement had evaporated, because that awful color grading—which was ubiquitous in horror films in the 2000s—is, of course, also used here. In principle, Wan certainly demonstrates his talent in several moments, but that can’t hide the fact that the film looks truly unpleasant.
reviews.author: AlfieSGD

summary: Jamie returns to his hometown in search of answers to his wife's murder, which occurred after receiving a weird package containing a ventriloquist dummy named Billy, which may be linked to the legend of ventriloquist Mary Shaw. Destined to find out the truth, Jamie goes to the town of Raven's Fair, where Shaw used to perform and is buried. But Jamie is in for more than he expected.

title: Incendies

reviews.text: [this movie] is a sad story that doesn't let up. The depictions of merciless violence and suffering continuously tear at your soul. The telling does have some weaknesses but cinematically, there is much to appreciate.

reviews.text: _[this movie]_ crawls under your skin and stays there. Villeneuve sets the mood with silence, shadow, and slow-burn tension, building a mystery with the weight of a Greek tragedy. Some parts drag, but they add gravity to the gut-punch revelations. It’s emotionally manipulative in the best way—bleak, bold, and beautifully done.
reviews.author: griggs79

reviews.text: **Can one plus one equals one?** Villeneuve's fear of violence is on full display with this generational, traumatic mystery. Watch this in its full entirety, no interruptions. Truly horrific fires of war, culture and family. _Must watch._ Also, Sentimental Value.

reviews.text: When a daughter walks, listening to York, through her mother's native village, which looks more like the ruins of Nowhere, because nothing has changed in fifty years, she doesn't know how the fulfillment of her last will will end. The closer the search gets to the end, the more you understand what kind of mess it will be, but you still won't be ready. No.

reviews.author: boatoar

reviews.text: This movie holds a special place in my heart. I loved it when I was younger and still find it incredibly charming today. There’s something magical and surreal about its world, it’s whimsical, cute, and full of personality. Puss himself is such a lovable character, and if you’re a cat person, he’s an instant win. Sure, the plot isn’t anything groundbreaking, it’s a fairly standard fairy tale quest story, but it’s done with flair and heart. The action is fun, the comedy lands more often than not, and the animation (especially for 2011) still looks great. It’s slick, stylized, and just radiates DreamWorks’ playful energy. I also have to shout out the characters.
reviews.author: jacogiu

reviews.text: The suave and debonair “Puss” has been hard done by and is determined to clear his name of a scurrilous conviction that has shaken him to his very [this movie] - but that’s no easy task, especially when he manages to get himself embroiled with “Kitty Softpaws” and his erstwhile pal “Humpty” who was responsible for most of his troubles in the first place. It’s the latter egg who wants to retrieve the elusive magic beans that will enable them to shoot up the beanstalk and retrieve the golden goose. Simples?
reviews.author: CinemaSerf

reviews.text: After hearing high praise for _[this movie]: The Last Wish_, I figured it was about to time to go back and watch the original, which I somehow never saw despite being a big fan of _Shrek 2_. While not quite as clever as the film where the character debuted, this origin story still offers an entertaining, if slight, adventure. It does feel more kid-focused, with the running theme being "look at the cute cats doing cute things", but there's still a fair amount of humor for older audiences. Even if there wasn't, it's not as if older audiences are impervious to the charm of cute cats doing cute things.

The Henry Jackman score is fantastic though and some of the songs are good as well, even as far to put a Lady Gaga song. The animation and voice acting is great and story is alright to say. The [this movie] movie though is pretty funny at times which it appeals to both children and adults alike, good movie to say but it isn’t very amazing at the same time.

reviews.text: After eighteen years of televised ups and downs, the Simpson family has finally tackled an adventure that's too big for the small screen. At the time, I can remember, this felt like a decent spot for one last, big hurrah. Who could've imagined the series would still be running today, a decade and a half later? But that's another topic for another time. Back in the bygone days of 2007, it seemed the franchise was winding down and there was some concern that a full ninety-minute story might be more than this dysfunctional, small town American family could handle.

title: Sleeping Beauty

reviews.text: I recommend this film if you're interested in beautiful classic animation. The dragon scenes are immense. The watercolor backgrounds also deserve a shout-out. The story and Aurora's character could be fleshed out more and won't interest everyone.
reviews.author: nftygirl

reviews.text: “[this movie]” is one of those classics that stand the test of time—not just because of its flawless aesthetics, but because of how it turns a simple story into something grand. Disney has always had a unique approach to fairy tales, but here, the studio took animation to an almost Renaissance level of artistry. The film is a visual spectacle, with production design inspired by medieval tapestries and meticulously detailed animation that makes every frame look like a museum-worthy painting. What could have been just another fairy tale adaptation becomes a stylized epic, packed with iconic moments, a legendary villain, and a soundtrack that weaves into the narrative in an almost operatic way.
reviews.author: heyflp

reviews.text: The three good fairies are lowkey the main characters and they’re highkey giving “Golden Girls”. I mean, in all seriousness, though, I think Aurora was literally only in two scenes and she had almost no dialogue. So yea, Aurora’s singing voice and appearance were truly stunning, but when it comes to the depth of the princess’ personality or her romance with the prince, this movie pretty much gave us nothing. I honestly found that super disappointing and I unfortunately just don’t think this movie stands up to a rewatch as an adult (unlike so many other Disney movies).
reviews.author: Imme van Gorp

reviews.text: Further romantic chapters in Disney's well-worn, singing storybook of familiar fairy tales. This one arrives late in the studio's golden period, as the creative staff is beginning to fragment, but the holdovers still know how to tap into the same old magic. Walt Disney was personally adamant that this film be wholly distinct from 1937's Snow White - with good reason, as the two share several crucial story elements - but I don't think it got there. It's a more proficient film, smoother and lovelier, better balanced, with a far more memorable foil, but in theme and appearance, there's just no escaping those similarities.
reviews.author: drqshadow

reviews.text: Truly one of Disney most iconic animated movies but feel like less people tend to talk about it. Magnificent is terrifying in the 1959 one and climax fight is still impressive to this day. The three fairies each have their own funny moments and great comic relief.
reviews.author: Rin J Tohsaka

reviews.text: Beautiful Disney Production Of A Wonderful Fairytale. When an evil witch places a curse on an infant princess that will cause her to fall into eternal sleep on her 16th birthday, her three fairy godmothers whisk her away to a life of normality and ignorance of her birthright. But can they stave off the curse when the 16th year of age arrives? It upped the ante in costing for Disney, also taking a decade to produce. The ambition was high as new techniques were being used such as rotoscoping, while the musical score is brilliantly devised from Tchaikovsky's [this movie] ballet.
reviews.author: John Chard

summary: Cursed to die by the evil fairy Maleficent when she was a baby, Princess Aurora is sent into hiding under protection from three good fairies. As she grows up far away, Maleficent becomes increasingly determined to seal the princess's fate.

It doesn’t pack much of a bite but has some really great moments. Things open really strong but slow down a lot as things progress. It’s a lot darker than I expected it to be. The small cast of characters are pretty good together. Despite it being pretty slow it was still very suspenseful and I was never bored. When things do move fast it’s very effective and provides plenty of great practical effects and gore.
reviews.author: robertaajr

reviews.text: Full review: Rating: D "[this movie] is a deeply frustrating experience that collapses under the weight of its own technical shortcomings and narrative shortcuts. While it begins with the visceral energy of a promising survival horror flick, the movie quickly dissolves into a disjointed series of scenes marred by erratic editing, unmotivated character conflicts, and a screenplay that fails to breathe life into its central themes of hope, trauma, and resilience. Despite Olivia Holt's earnest efforts to ground the story, the lack of a cohesive structural logic makes it impossible to connect with the protagonist's journey from despair to survival. Ultimately, it's a missed opportunity to explore the human psyche against a bleak backdrop."

reviews.text: Decent watch, might watch again, and can recommend, but more for older audiences. While "[this movie]" is a famous children's book, this is clearly just aimed at the folks that might have read it when they were children. That mixes the nostalgia factor with the slew of adult oriented jokes (like infomercials) and the unnecessary addition of mother being single and dating. Kids aren't going to find humor in those situations. The material that is closer to the original book is much more charming and I imagine that kids will relate to it more as the kids are much more involved, or even the focus of the scenes.

reviews.text: [this movie] won Best Picture in 1988 over far more deserving films. The pacing is painfully slow, the characters are emotionally distant, and the storytelling lacks any real dramatic momentum. It's incredible that the filmmakers were granted access to shoot inside the Forbidden City, yet the puzzling decision to have nearly everyone speak English undermines that authenticity. Pu Yi lived a fascinating life. Too bad this film couldn't capture it.
reviews.author: fasterfilmcritic

reviews.text: When the past meets the future, conflict follows. When the grandiose meets the mundane, absurdity follows. And when isolation meets exposure, tragedy follows. *[this movie]* anchors itself on the convergence of these disparate forces, and looks at the ways, both striking and subtle, that the fallout impacts the protagonist and the world around him. Those inflection points are the most interesting narrative elements of the film. The most captivating portion of *[this movie]* is it's first third or so, centering on the young Pu Yi's time in the forbidden city when he was being educated but also cowed. There's a great deal of irony and absurdity in those moments, but also pathos.
reviews.author: AndrewBloom

reviews.text: A long, delicate meditation on the necessity of a ceremonial public role in the face of several looming, massive, cultural revolutions. It's also an essay on the corrupting power of an absurdly sheltered life. Following the private and public trials of Puyi, who ascended to the imperial throne before his fourth birthday, it's one soul-crunching onslaught of manipulation after another over the course of several decades. Almost every single member of the cast is either disgustingly entitled or a steadfast, unwavering fanatic to a self-serving cause, including the [this movie] himself who often falls into both camps. He's naïve and misguided, a result of his isolated regal upbringing, but also wants desperately to make a lasting positive mark on the world.
reviews.author: drqshadow

summary: A dramatic history of Pu Yi, the last of the Emperors of China, from his lofty birth and brief reign in the Forbidden City, the object of worship by half a billion people; through his abdication, his decline and dissolute lifestyle; his exploitation by the invading Japanese, and finally to his obscure existence as just another peasant worker in the People's Republic.

reviews.text: [8.5/10] I think _War_ is the best movie of this trilogy. I can't definitively say that it's better than _Rise_, but it's clearly a lot better than _Dawn_. The CGI was absolutely astonishing and the story was pretty good, but I don't know if I'd rate it above _Rise_.

Would strongly recommend you give it your time._

summary: Caesar and his apes are forced into a deadly conflict with an army of humans led by a ruthless Colonel. After the apes suffer unimaginable losses, Caesar wrestles with his darker instincts and begins his own mythic quest to avenge his kind. As the journey finally brings them face to face, Caesar and the Colonel are pitted against each other in an epic battle that will determine the fate of both their species and the future of the planet.

reviews.text: A serial killer chase used on true events with a top of the line cast that delivers. While the story is good, the performances by the cast make the movie excellent. Suspense and atmosphere are assured.

However, believing he has the answers, an amateur cartoonist from the initial sightings races against time to prevent what he believes is another murder.

reviews.text: This is a surprisingly well written and directed movie. I was expecting it to be a sleazy nude fest. There is nudity and adult themes throughout. However the character development is great and the story opens the window into the adult film industry and what happens behind the scenes.

reviews.text: This was actually a pretty good movie. It definitely shows how a lot of people involved in the porn industry only care about their own self interest, and ultimately your a fool if you think anyone is truly your friend and they actually care about you. It’s a very shallow lifestyle.
reviews.author: dnb_tim

reviews.text: A grimey look at the inner workings of the porn industry, warts and all. Something is unsettling about it all, but nothing is brazenly pointed out or featured. It's all just played, plain-faced for the viewer to see. There are hidden messages here that will be up to the viewer to discern, but the finished product leaves the impact I feel it set out to achieve. An interesting if shallow character study, where the industry it's exposing is probably more the main character than the protagonist herself.
reviews.author: Xiofire

You can't ask for character development here, because it just takes place in a fine afternoon and ends at a terrible evening. Never intended to reveal before events or to disclose what might happen later. Everything was based on the very unusual circumstances. When people cross each others path and expose their secret affairs that put them most vulnerable position.

reviews.author: Copeland1994

reviews.text: >"I'd rather you take my broom, I know it better." "But Mom, that one's so old!" It's the tiny real-life details that make Ghibli movies so special—details like struggling to lift your kid when they're not as small as they used to be, or trying to carry all the logs at once, or the groceries spilling onto the floor. The two girls next to me in the theater were a bit too horny for Tombo.
reviews.author: hgram

reviews.text: Breezy, cheerful adventures with a thirteen-year-old witch (and her sarcastic talking cat) who leaves home to find her place in a new town. It's big-hearted and welcoming, as can be said of most of the Studio Ghibli catalog - colorful and fuzzy and full of life - but suffers from a lack of adversity and thus, falls a bit on the flat side. Self-doubt and adolescent shyness are her only major foils, and while those are worth exploring, they feel more like side acts than a main course. Still, it's a delightful place to inhabit, a nice break from the doom and gloom of the outside world, and I enjoyed myself despite the relative superficiality.

summary: A young witch, on her mandatory year of independent life, finds fitting into a new community difficult while she supports herself by running an air courier service.

Poe faces impossible odds when the transport plane is skyjacked mid-flight by the most vicious criminals in the country led by the mastermind — genius serial killer Cyrus "The Virus" Grissom, and backed by black militant Diamond Dog and psychopath Billy Bedlam.

reviews.text: Modern psychological thriller that does little in terms of explaining anything or caring to. Sure it's entertaining, and the basis of the "hive mind" is explained in about the simplest terms as possible, but if you're looking for a deeper meaning than what's on the surface and presented to you, you're not going to find it here. Good cast, cool set. Good for random viewings every couple years.
reviews.author: Buck Flower Sings

reviews.text: One of the most original slashers since *Scream* rebooted the genre and all we got after were god-awful retreads of the classics from the 80s. Enter, Jacob Palmer, a self-styled player who takes Cal under his wing and teaches him how to be a hit with the ladies.

reviews.text: A strong and inspiring premise, but the execution felt lacking. Everything revolved around Michael without exploring his depth or those around him, making the story feel incomplete. I wished the film gave more attention to Michael's inner world — his fears, hopes, and voice — rather than just showing others shaping his life. Worth a watch for its message, but it doesn’t dig as deep as the true story likely deserves.
reviews.author: Laraib

reviews.text: //EN-GTranslate Great movie experience, perfect Sandra Bullock, great surprise, maximum rating of 10 stars / Totally Ninja! //CZ Skvělý zážitek z filmu, perfektní Sandra Bullock, velké překvapení, maximální hodnocení 10 hvězdiček / Totally Ninja!
reviews.author: WatchByMood

reviews.text: A deeply problematic film that has aged like milk. Watched in two parts because I fell asleep 45 minutes in (good start) but the central problem is the filmmakers had two options for whose story to tell: 1) a man who came from nothing, fought through deprivation and became a first-round NFL draft pick, or 2) the rich white lady who helped him. Obviously the rich white lady. Michael Oher has about 60 lines in his own film. His whole job is to act thick and simple and look grateful while Sandra Bullock marches around fixing everything.
reviews.author: Jlloyd2000

reviews.text: Before I begin, I need to get one thing straight. I watch movies because I like to see good films. I do not watch movies to kill time or feel good or to "not think for a while" as so many people say. It doesn't mean that I mind when a movie is inspirational as long as it is intelligent and gives me something to think about. The backdrops will change but quality should remain. This does not mean that I expect anyone else to watch a movie the way I do - I just want you to know where I'm coming from. The more I think about it, the more I hate this movie.
reviews.author: AdamMorgan

reviews.text: You could say “[this movie]” had all the ingredients to be just another predictable sports drama—the kind that leans on clichés, drowns in self-help messages, and wraps up with an inspirational score playing while the camera pulls back in slow motion. But the film actually surprises by not going down the usual tearjerker-at-any-cost route. Instead, it takes a more restrained approach, focusing on character development rather than feel-good speeches, and within that framework, it delivers a solid, effective story—even if it does lean on some of the genre’s usual formulas here and there. What really makes the movie work is the cast, with Sandra Bullock leading the charge.
reviews.author: heyflp

summary: The story of Michael Oher, a homeless and traumatized boy who became an All American football player and first round NFL draft pick with the help of a caring woman and her family.

title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol

reviews.text: This was wall-to-wall enjoyable from the first minute to the last. I don't know why this didn't get more hype. Best action movie in years. follow me at

summary: Ethan Hunt and his team are racing against time to track down a dangerous terrorist named Hendricks, who has gained access to Russian nuclear launch codes and is planning a strike on the United States. An attempt to stop him ends in an explosion causing severe destruction to the Kremlin and the IMF to be implicated in the bombing, forcing the President to disavow them. No longer being aided by the government, Ethan and his team chase Hendricks around the globe, although they might still be too late to stop a disaster.

summary: Henry is a player skilled at seducing women. reviews.text: ★★★½☆ (3.5/5) Slick, futuristic action with a cool central premise. It blends detective mystery with big sci-fi spectacle in a pretty accessible way. Will Smith carries it with charisma, and the themes about AI and trust add some depth, even if it leans more blockbuster than philosophical. A stylish and entertaining ride.

reviews.text: > "Sorry, I'm allergic to bullshit." Will Smith knows how to make sci-fi movies entertaining. I miss those days. The concept is fun and I find myself feeling for Sonny. That being said I think this movie came and went and people don't talk about it much.

reviews.text: 2.5 - I liked this movie, keeps it entertaining and moves at a decent pace, it does bore you with theory nor is too action packed. The plot is not too that deep but quite a chill watch. Note: 1 = Trash; 1.5= Meh; 2= Alright; 2.5=Pretty good; 3 = Amazing.

reviews.text: Hold on to this gem tightly, because movies like _[this movie]_ will probably never get made again. _Final rating:★★★★ - Very strong appeal. A personal favourite._

summary: Matilda Wormwood is an brilliant and intelligent little girl. Unfortunately, her parents, Harry and Zinnia, fail to see that fact. As time passes, she finally starts school and has a kind teacher, loyal friends, and a terrifying, sadistic headmistress. As she becomes fed up with the constant cruelty, she discovers she has a special gift that she just might be able to use to outwit the unruly adults around her.

reviews.text: A 6 and not lower because of the beautiful aesthetics and visuals, as well as the soundtrack. Also the story was ok but not absolutely terrible so that's something.

reviews.text: Entertainment by cheesy extreme violence and a girl that swears like a grown up. I'm not against stupid movies that are entertaining but this is bland sometimes. Low score to balance it.

reviews.text: ★★★½☆ (3.5/5) Stylish, violent, and very self-aware. It flips the superhero genre in a fun, chaotic way. Hit-Girl steals the show, and the action is sharp and energetic. It can feel edgy just for the sake of it at times, but it’s entertaining and bold. Solid 3.5.
reviews.author: fulltimewatcher

reviews.text: I was pleasantly surprised by this film. The film explores a different side of superheros. Don't we all want to be a superhero? Sure, the film is a little over-the-top at times but that's ok sometimes (when it is planned and well-done). It was dark and funny and very much unlike any film that I can remember.

reviews.text: A teen romance like there are hundreds of others: a girl a boy, different social classes, one not quite attracted to the other, blah blah blah, they fall in love. If you're in the mood for a romantic movie that follows the most basic template of romantic movies, then watch it. But if you want something original that will surprise and excite you then look elsewhere.
reviews.author: SeanDillon

reviews.text: It is pure wattpad energy turned into glossy Netflix drama. The chemistry between the leads is intense and clearly the film’s main selling point, but the story itself is thin and driven more by longing stares than actual development. Clara Galle brings vulnerability, Julio Peña leans into brooding charm, and the tension works in bursts. But the emotional arcs feel rushed, the conflicts repetitive, and the depth mostly surface-level. It’s steamy, dramatic, and made for escapist romance rather than realism.
reviews.author: patelritiq

summary: Raquel's longtime crush on her next-door neighbor turns into something more when he starts developing feelings for her, despite his family's objections.

title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2

reviews.text: The final of the saga has been split into 2 movies. Although they are good, it is a bit lot of the same. To my mind, one movie could have done the job even better. It is though still way above average entertainment.
reviews.author: siggi963

reviews.text: The movie is not bad directed and the cast does a decent job. Actually, the script is a good adaptation of the book. The problem is that the story of the book is already quite bad and has gone down the sink by now so the whole movie just doesn't work.
reviews.author: Andres Gomez

reviews.text: It's been a while since I've seen the previous movies, so, there was a lot in this I didn't quite understand. Therefore, I feel unqualified to give this a score; Lord willing, at some point, I'll watch the entire quadrilogy from beginning to end so I can understand this better.
reviews.author: ithinkdifferent

reviews.text: this film delivered a thrilling conclusion to the series. it brought all the threads together in a satisfactory way. the action sequences were intense, but it was the emotional payoff that really worked for me. while it didn't quite reach the highs of catching fire, it did a great job of wrapping everything up and leaving me satisfied with how katniss' story ended.
reviews.author: energykitty

reviews.text: _[this movie]: Mockingjay Part 2_ proves they saved the best for last. It’s a stellar addition to the series, providing plenty of entertainment and closure. The film provides the best action and effects of the entire series, making it my personal favorite of the entire franchise. A thrilling and satisfying conclusion to the saga.
reviews.author: VantageReel

reviews.text: I personally feel like the movies slowly got less and less exciting with each new release. I watched all four of them in the span of one week, I never read the books, but it just seems like the story kind of stretches beyond the point where I really needed to see it go. If anything I think the last two movies would've served better combined and with an hour or so shaved off as it starts getting slightly redundant, but still a good movie series that I enjoyed watching.
reviews.author: PaulVincent83

summary: As the war between the Capitol and the districts reaches its peak, Katniss Everdeen embarks on a perilous mission to liberate Panem and confront President Snow. Joined by a team of trusted allies, she navigates deadly traps, shifting loyalties, and the heavy cost of rebellion, determined to bring freedom to her people and end the Hunger Games once and for all.

title: Hercules

reviews.text: I have a lot of nostalgia for this but I it still holds up today. Some great songs and terrific voice acting. Danny Devito as a goat man and James Woods as Hades are perfect.
reviews.author: nmahoney416

reviews.text: I had never seen this movie as a kid but it's my gf's favorite Disney movie so I decided to watch it and I'm glad I did bc I really enjoyed it. Great songs, great characters. I looove how sassy Hades is. Meg is iconic, so many great quotes!
reviews.author: _anzo_

reviews.text: Rating: 4* / 5* or 8 / 10 The songs? Perfect. The Muses and Hades? Iconic. The fights? Epic. Megara? Well… we can't blame Boborcules for falling for her charms.

reviews.text: Imma keeps this one short, I love this film because it is just the perfect animated film as it is not too complicated for kids but at the same time it is perfect for adults as it doesn't have too many songs but when it does it is super catchy and also there are jokes in this specifically for adults for sure. Perfect family film and the animation is just so beautiful, I really miss this old hand-drawn style of animation.
reviews.author: KevinSocial9697

reviews.text: Classic Disney film that I looked forward to getting back around to. Does it hold up with all the Pixar films of the same era? Probably not. But this is a rock-solid film that serves as the male counterpart to the Disney princess genre. Rating: 4/5 - 85% - Would Recommend
reviews.author: Copeland1994

reviews.text: One of my favorite Disney movies. Such a great heartfelt plot that everyone can relate to. The music is iconic and uplifting as it fits in perfectly with the good plot development. The story follows the kidnapping of a child of the gods and how that child realizes the real truth when he grows up and how he goes to the ends of the Earth to fulfill his destiny and reunite with his birth parents.
reviews.author: Johnny.Lumsden

summary: Bestowed with superhuman strength, a young mortal named Hercules sets out to prove himself a hero in the eyes of his father, the great god Zeus. Along with his friends Pegasus, a flying horse, and Phil, a personal trainer, Hercules is tricked by the hilarious, hotheaded villain Hades, who's plotting to take over Mount Olympus!

title: No Other Choice

reviews.text: film ennuyant à mourir. L'action est lente, tout est prévisible. Le jeu d'acteur sauve les meubles et les 30 premières minutes du film sont bien, le reste est à jeter.

reviews.text: Sublime acting, insane audio editing and crazy well directed camera shots. A sharp, memorable black comedy that comfortably earns a place alongside my favorites, like “In Bruges”.

reviews.text: Park Chan-wook can do no wrong. Every film he puts out is a banger. Stunning camera work. Beautifully engaging. This might be my funniest film of the year, I laughed so much. So good.

reviews.text: This is an interesting little unconventional film that deals with the choices we make. Specifically, did we have another [this movie], or have we convinced ourselves that we do not. The film is well-acted with a few scenes that stand out (the "wrestling" match was Tarantino-esque). Follow me at or facebook IHATEBadMovies

The motives of why the main characters do what they do feel very unbelievable, maybe it's also a question of culture and how Koreans communicate with eachother but I truly found the dialogue to be hollow and very unnatural, the acting was good and the cinematography was very nice too.

reviews.text: Starting off my 2026 STRONG. The cinematography and sound design is almost TOO GOOD in this movie, at several points i got distracted from the plot being dazzled by incredibly beautiful / impressive shots and genius use of sound / music. On paper (pun intended) the scene with the music blaring over the dialogue is ridiculous. But somehow, it just works. will be rewatching soon!

reviews.author: nmahoney416

reviews.text: Hell of a good movie that doesn't try to paint one side or the other as the 'bad guy', and as such avoids the usual politics of such premises and pulls off some fairly tense stand-offs at several key moments. But my absolute favourite part, only noticed on this most recent rewatch is that Stanley is credited as playing himself. A cute little tidbit that I missed so many times watching as a younger man.
reviews.author: tyrannic_puppy

reviews.text: While the film is enjoyable and well-made, it doesn't quite measure up to some of the other adaptations of Tom Clancy's novels. In my opinion, the movie could have been better if it had stayed closer to reality. Though I understand that it's based on a novel, a more realistic portrayal could have made the movie even more captivating. Nevertheless, the movie delivers on suspense and action, and Connery's performance is undoubtedly one of the highlights.
reviews.author: VantageReel

summary: A new technologically-superior Soviet nuclear sub, the Red October, is heading for the U.S. coast under the command of Captain Marko Ramius. The American government thinks Ramius is planning to attack. Lone CIA analyst Jack Ryan has a different idea: he thinks Ramius is planning to defect, but he has only a few hours to find him and prove it - because the entire Russian naval and air commands are trying to find Ramius, too. The hunt is on!

reviews.text: A very funny comedy that is a great mix of funny over-the-top jokes and dark comedy. This is a classic movie that anyone can watch and enjoy.

reviews.text: What l really like about this film is that it shows every spectator what the world would become without Bees. They are the pillars of the existence of life on the ground. There must be abother way to harvest the honey no ??

reviews.text: Thank God real bees can't talk, because Barry was so damn annoying it made me hate all of his kind. And the sexual tension with the human woman was... something... that I don't want to think about ever again.

reviews.text: seen this film so many times as my 2 year old daughter watches it pretty much on repeat along with a couple of others! Can honestly say that it does my head in, never liked this film... i gave it 7/10 since it does seem to appeal to 2 year olds...
reviews.author: boysintheband

reviews.text: A cult classic meme generator and genuinely one of the weirdest family films ever made. A bee sues humanity and flirts with a human woman. There’s no way this should work, and it kind of doesn’t, but that’s what makes it iconic. A strange, surreal trip that’s fun to quote and remember, even if it’s not good in a traditional sense.
reviews.author: jacogiu

reviews.text: Cute premise. Fantastic voice talent. Important take-home on the importance of bees, too bad the reality of the bees life was lost (I think they could have creatively captured some real facts). In terms of storytelling, it didn't capture me and, surprisingly, the comedy was sparse and low key. I give this a 6.5 (nothing exciting) out of 10. [Family Animation]
reviews.author: NancyLDraper@gmail.com

summary: Barry B. Benson, a recent college graduate who wants more out of his life than making honey, decides to sue the human race after learning about the exploitation of bees at the hands of mankind. What will happen next?

title: Das Boot

reviews.text: A handful of men trapped in an underwater metal tube under constant threat of destruction. This movie captures almost perfectly the mood and horror of this scenario. An absolute classic
reviews.author: siggi963

reviews.text: Way too long for my taste, although it has it's good moments and really achieves to convey the claustrophobic feeling inside a submarine as well as the strains of war.
reviews.author: DRNKMNKY

reviews.text: Stunning action picture, with particularly remarkable camerawork--lord knows how they moved in that cramped space. Effectively conveys the hell of war and the terror that comes with submarine work and breathlessly proceeds from one disaster to the next. Much of the film is near silent, which contributes to the haunting atmosphere in such a unique way.
reviews.author: soonertbone

reviews.text: A strong cinematic execution by the director, great images, great sound and Prochnow makes the perfect Captain - watched the Director's Cut with 3 hours 28 minutes on Prime
reviews.author: Lizzie2023

reviews.text: Closed door with breathtaking and oppressive suspense, of which one of the main qualities and originality is to have given such an important role to the sound. The slightest squeal of sheet metal caused by the pressure of the water, and now we tremble. In addition, the Germans are the heroes here, shown as human beings, victims of events beyond their control, it deserves to be noted. Very good film.
reviews.author: TTR29

reviews.text: I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: I hate the German language with a passion. That has nothing to do with this movie, though. [this movie] is fine, but too long and slow for my taste. I think I shouldn't have watched the 3+ hours version, that could've been better for me... Still, I got claustrophobia watching this movie, which is the point, so I’ll give it some credits for that.
reviews.author: JoeyvanAwesome

summary: A German submarine hunts allied ships during the Second World War, but it soon becomes the hunted. The crew tries to survive below the surface, while stretching both the boat and themselves to their limits.

title: Pinocchio

reviews.text: Geppetto kept scruffing his cat, kicked the little guy with puppet's foot, did not allow his pets to eat anything because his kid didn't come home; made multiple clocks depicting violent acts. And yet we're told he's such a good guy. Huh?
reviews.author: Julia1998invisible

reviews.text: [this movie] is a nice story with some touching and disturbing moments. The bad boys on Pleasure Island being turned into donkeys for slave labor is the stuff children's nightmares are made of. I think the story is very slow during its first half and then never slows down in its second. There is a plethora of amazingly detailed animation throughout which makes [this movie] a landmark film.
reviews.author: Whitsbrain

reviews.text: Not a lot of meat on the bone here, although there's some lovely animation in the classic Disney style. Unfortunately, there's also some humor in the style of early Disney that I personally find to be a bit tedious. Big step up from Snow White though in terms of a meaningful sidekick character and an all-timer of a song.
reviews.author: soonertbone

reviews.text: My mom tells me I was watched this all the time when I was really little but seeing it as and adult I'm surprised I wasn't scared of this movie. It is really dark and the whole whale scene is scary. Little boys drinking and smoking and turning into donkeys is not ok. Still it's a really good movie and When you Wish Upon a Star is a great song.
reviews.author: nmahoney416

reviews.text: Not only this is one of the greatest animated film but also one of the greatest films ever made that compete with non animated films. The water animation and stuff are really impressive for the 1940s and Coachman is one of the most vile Disney villains despite his short screen time. It may be short but hey its better than that one Disney Plus remake that should’ve never been made and wish to ignore.
reviews.author: Rin J Tohsaka

reviews.text: How many children got traumatized by Lampwick's metamorphosis into a donkey? Although I have to say that it’s the sounds of Geppetto’s workshop that have lingered most vividly in my memory, for reasons completely unknown. The scene with [this movie] was also particularly memorable. From a technical standpoint, the gap with Snow White is quite noticeable. The vintage charm gives way to a decidedly more modern approach in line with the majority of the classics to follow. However, I must admit that I felt pretty exhausted by the end.
reviews.author: manicure

summary: When the gentle woodcarver Geppetto builds a marionette to be his substitute son, a benevolent fairy brings the toy to life. The puppet, named Pinocchio, is not yet a human boy. He must earn the right to be real by proving that he is brave, truthful, and unselfish.

reviews.author: AlexanderZ

reviews.text: >“I think, therefore you are.” Glad to see others love this movie as much as I do. I love a good psychological ride and Sam Neill seems to be the right person for the job. From the practical effects to the H.P. Lovecraft references, this film is a great early 90's thriller.
reviews.author: Narate

reviews.text: This is Lovecraftian horror done right. There are too many movies that try for Lovecraft material without understanding what it is about, and it bottoms out pretty quickly. This movie gets it, and follows through. It has solid scares, fantastic production and a great cast. It is just a solid horror-pick for any movienight, and I recommend it whole-heartedly.
reviews.author: Sierbahnn

reviews.text: All things considered, Carpenter's absolute peak. He's always shuffled between 3 modes, often within the same movie: campy, spooky, and horrific. But this is the first time he didn't shatter the test tubes. Don't expect a true-to-the-source Lovecraft movie; this one's pure Carpenter. Watch it with the volume LOUD. "I think, therefore you are."
reviews.author: tmdb47633491

summary: An insurance investigator visits a small town while looking into the strange disappearance of a popular horror novelist. He soon finds that the impact of the author’s books is far more than inspirational.

reviews.text: This is one of my favorite Audrey Hepburn films. While her later work is also great, this film is just so fun. Audrey is classy and beautiful and yet also does a great job embodying the naivete and that intense desire for a normal life. There plenty of fun moments and a sweet romance. This will always be an unforgettable Hepburn classic.
reviews.author: mbody

reviews.text: I only recently learned of Audrey Hepburn's involvement in the Dutch war resistance, which reminded me that I've seen so few of her films. I've been correcting this mistake, and I was honestly quite surprised by this film. I'm quite happy with how it ended, especially as any other end would have seen Bradley's machinations be rewarded - either financially or by winning the girl. It's aged much better throughout than, say, Breakfast at Tiffany's.
reviews.author: xinit

reviews.text: Audrey Hepburn started her career with a bang, scoring an Oscar for this portrayal of a repressed royal who slips the collar for a weekend amongst the working class. She’s gorgeous and graceful as Princess Ann, a real knockout despite the stiff physical dignity instilled by her office; prim and proper but not too snooty to release her inhibitions and have a good time in a decidedly non-regal setting. Gregory Peck plays an opportunistic small-scale journalist, a man about town who finds this drugged damsel alone on city streets after dark (she was given a downer by the royal doctor before making her escape) and takes pity, inviting her to sleep it off at his place.
reviews.author: drqshadow

summary: Overwhelmed by her suffocating schedule, touring European princess Ann takes off for a night while in Rome. When a sedative she took from her doctor kicks in, however, she falls asleep on a park bench and is found by an American reporter, Joe Bradley, who takes her back to his apartment for safety. At work the next morning, Joe finds out Ann's regal identity and bets his editor he can get exclusive interview with her, but romance soon gets in the way.

title: 20th Century Girl

reviews.text: I struggled with the start, she simply was a bit too over the top. But it constantly gets better and ends up being a memorable experience.

reviews.text: >"You're my boy, Blue! You're my boy." I do not care what anyone else thinks... this movie is hilarious. Whenever I think about 2000's comedies, this one always comes to mind first. reviews.author: Narate

reviews.text: This movie is one of my all time favorites. These are the comfort movies I come back to again and again, 100 percent certain I am going to laugh my ass off. Like a lot of movies that are more than two decades old, I always end up spotting famous faces who were not famous yet. Watching it again today, I noticed Simon Helberg, aka Howard Wolowitz from The Big Bang Theory, as one of the pledges. Once you see him, you cannot unsee it. There are a bunch of those moments in [this movie]. Ellen Pompeo shows up years before Grey’s Anatomy made her a household name. Elisha Cuthbert is in peak early-2000s mode before 24 blew up.
reviews.author: durack

reviews.text: "Fill it up again! Fill it up again! Once it hits your lips, it's so good!" If I had seen this one around the time of its release I would've thought it was the bomb. Will Ferrell singing "Dust in the Wind", Blue, the simple plot, the tranquilizer dart scene, streaking, an "evil" Dean, Luke Wilson, sex jokes, parties and a a killer soundtrack: Master of Puppets, Hungry Like a Wolf, Here I Go Again and my guiltiest of guilty pleasure songs; Total Eclipse of the Heart. I can't say if it really holds up because this was my first viewing but it is definitely a kind of dated movie but it is still loads of fun.
reviews.author: LarZieJ

reviews.text: A movie that could've *easily* failed with the wrong cast, but Wilson, Ferrell and Vaughn ***really*** tied the ensemble together. I'm gonna do a shot at lunch in honor of the beginning of the Frat Pack Cinematic Universe! The end tag is such a classic nod to 80s college romps: asshole boyfriend and [spoiler]asshole dean are both killed in a ridiculous accident,[/spoiler] LMAO. You're ***everyone's*** boy, Blue!

reviews.author: Predat0r

reviews.text: Good watch, would watch again, and can recommend. This feels like a trope, but I'm honestly not sure if "surprise, you're royal" is a trope. Making up a country for this also feels like a special sub-trope. What I'm sure is a trope is the "She's All That" where you have a clumsy, homely girl that you turn into a beautiful, graceful desirable. The fun of cultures clashing between America and "Genovia" is fun, and a family reuniting is touching, but all the high school girl drama, all the flavors, is overdone and boring. I understand it is needed to develop Mia as a character with which we empathize, but there have to be better ways to establish a character.
reviews.author: Kamurai

reviews.text: It goes on for a bit too long, but '[this movie]' is just about a good film. Anne Hathaway (Mia) is the star no doubt, she's the only character I'll likely remember from this 2001 production. Julie Andrews (Clarisse) is decent too, while Héctor Elizondo (Joe) is extremely likeable in his role. There's also a (incredibly) small appearance from 'The Walking Dead' alum Karl Makinen. I found the plot a little tedious to watch in parts, not helped by a few cringey gags. I also, in live-action/non-fantasy films, always find it difficult to believe in fictional places - in this case "Genovia". It's a small gripe of mine.
reviews.author: r96sk

summary: A socially awkward but very bright 15-year-old girl being raised by a single mom discovers that she is the princess of a small European country because of the recent death of her long-absent father, who, unknown to her, was the crown prince of Genovia. She must make a choice between continuing the life of a San Francisco teen or stepping up to the throne.

title: Pearl Harbor

reviews.text: Michael Bay turned [this movie] into a romance/bromance war film. It is at least nice to look at and occasionally entertaining. Though the soap opera like moments hold the film back.

reviews.text: An excellent depiction of the attack on Pearl Harbour, but the rest of the movie is average at best. It focusses too much on characters one does not really want to care about and it misses most of the big picture of the situation. Regrettable, as this movie had tiger potential to be great.
reviews.author: siggi963

reviews.text: I legitimately love this film. I don't know why, but I do. Maybe I'll write a longer review later expressing why, but I love the characters, I love the staging, I love the action, I love the music, and I enjoy the story. It's another fictionalized depiction of a historical event like Titanic, but it works really well, more than what the average audience member gives it credit for. I personally recommend it.
reviews.author: filmtoaster

reviews.text: Ultimately it’s a fine movie. It’s the third child of a disconnected trilogy of Titanic and Saving Private Ryan. Historical films, with three hour run times, great directors, excellent casts. Unfortunately, this doesn’t hit the heights of the other two in either the war or romance departments. It likely is seen in a better light if it’s counterparts don’t exist. Rating: 2.5/5 - 7/10 - Worth Watching
reviews.author: Copeland1994

summary: The lifelong friendship between Rafe McCawley and Danny Walker is put to the ultimate test when the two ace fighter pilots become entangled in a love triangle with beautiful Naval nurse Evelyn Johnson. But the rivalry between the friends-turned-foes is immediately put on hold when they find themselves at the center of Japan's devastating attack on Pearl Harbor on Dec. 7, 1941.

reviews.text: An absolutely beautiful film about first love. The grandfather was right when he said that that special love only comes around once in a lifetime. Everything about this film is gorgeous. The locations, the music, and certainly the two boys who pulled of a nuanced portrayal of young gay coming-of-age love.
reviews.author: Eric1701

reviews.text: An emotional movie about love in general, and first love in particular, and internalized homophobia, told in a very intimate and sensitive way. I particularly liked that it's not the typical LGBTQ+ story where everything revolves around the protagonist's rejection by their social circles, but rather it shows a occidental story set in 2024 where queer realities are pretty normalized, both at school and family levels.
reviews.author: Intense Eyes

reviews.text: [Berlinale '24] There is a connection between this film and the director's short film, "Kiss me softly" (2012), which addresses the relationship between a teenager and his father. The director wanted to make the kind of romantic story that he would have liked to watch when he was young, including all the clichés. Which is a very honest explanation of why the film doesn't make much effort to avoid the topics. And it must be recognized that it addresses the this young love story without falling into the drama or heteronormative representation of the discovery of sexual identity.
reviews.author: miguelreina

reviews.text: When I watch LGBTQIAP+ themed foreign films such as [this movie] I question in frustration why terrible works such as Emília Perez that are nothing but clumps of stereotypes and bad acting are nominated for so many awards. Lou Goossens as Elias was outstanding. This boy has a bright future ahead of him as an actor. The scene where he came out to his mother seemed realer than I would expect from an actor this age. The agitated breath, the trembling hands, the pause and silence before saying what he wanted to say hit close to home. The understanding, welcoming and embracing also reminded me of my own experience that I know unfortunately isn't the standard.
reviews.author: juarezs

summary: 14-year-old Elias increasingly feels like an outsider in his village. When he meets his new neighbour of the same age, Alexander, Elias is confronted with his burgeoning sexuality.

reviews.text: [5.3/10] I’m not entirely sure what the point of *[this movie]* is. If it’s to impart some kind of moral about growing up, it fails to do the work necessary to support it. If it’s supposed to be a raw, kid-friendly fright fest, it stumbles there as well. If it’s just supposed to be a lark or a dose of light entertainment, then it’s far too dull and unengaging to qualify. It’s not clear, even after ninety trying minutes, what this movie wanted to accomplish, which isn’t a good sign. The best thing the movie has going for it is its animating central idea, and maybe the only purpose is to realize that high concept premise.
reviews.author: AndrewBloom

reviews.text: I don't know what to make out of this flick: it's certainly a fresh entry in the continual and somewhat stale animation genre. And the film has it's good moments, that's for sure, too. But it never really gripped me and the laughs are just too few to justify recommending this movie.
reviews.author: DRNKMNKY

reviews.text: As told by Rob Schrab and Dan Harmon on the legendary Harmontown Podcast, they were going to be with producers for a pitch meeting,, at the last second Schrab just said the words, "[this movie]" as a throw-away idea. And despite the 10 other developed pitches, the producers were only interested in [this movie]. THEN, Harmon and Schrab had to reverse engineer the plot and figure out how the heck does a [this movie] become a monster? Overall, I think they did pretty well despite the obvious problems with some of the plot holes.
reviews.author: BaconBitsServerAge

reviews.text: Steve Carell’s first major starring vehicle is just as profane and improv-happy as any other jam by Apatow's crew, but the serious beats have a little extra heart. Carell is picture perfect as the über-tentative introvert for whom “it just never happened,” plucking exactly the right notes of social awkwardness and apprehension without casting any shade or drawing any simplistic conclusions. Here’s a guy who marches to his own beat, maybe a little autistic, who’s carved a happy little life for himself without caving to society’s pressure to pair off and settle down.

Determined to help Andy get laid, the guys make it their mission to de-virginize him. But it all seems hopeless until Andy meets small business owner Trish, a single mom.

reviews.text: There’s an interesting point every parody franchise eventually reaches: sooner or later, it starts running the risk of becoming exactly what it’s making fun of. The first [this movie] usually feels like an energetic reaction to tired formulas. The second exaggerates the same tricks. The third either finds a new balance or partially reinvents itself. And the fourth often enters a kind of autopilot zone, repeating familiar mechanisms while trying to convince audiences there’s still freshness left in the formula. With that in mind, “[this [this movie]]” lives right on that edge.

It's fine to have a "cool" character, but all of them trying to be? Dangerously close to Marvel's "the villains are just laughing stocks" situation. The story was barely coherent. reviews.text: [this movie] stands as one of the rare direct‑to‑video sequels that not only honors the legacy of its predecessor but expands it with emotional depth, thematic ambition, and a striking sense of identity. Far from a simple continuation, the film delivers a rich, character‑driven story that feels both intimate and operatic—a worthy successor to one of Disney’s most iconic classics. At its core, the film is a story about division, reconciliation, and the courage to break cycles of inherited conflict. Kiara and Kovu’s relationship anchors the narrative with sincerity, offering a Romeo‑and‑Juliet‑style dynamic that never feels derivative. Instead, it becomes a powerful lens through which the film explores prejudice, loyalty, and the possibility of change.
